| `descriptionZh` | `text` (nullable) | Chinese description, max 500 |
| `visibility` | `collection_visibility` enum (`private`, `public`) `not null default 'private'` | New enum |
| `publishedAt` | `timestamp({ withTimezone: true })` (nullable) | Set the first time `visibility` flips to `public`; used for browse sort |
| `updatedAt` | `timestamp({ withTimezone: true }) not null default now()` | Bumped on item add/remove or metadata edit; matches the pattern in `extensions` |

New enum at top of file: `export const collectionVisibilityEnum = pgEnum("collection_visibility", ["private", "public"])`.

Indexes:

- `idx_collections_owner_user` on `(ownerUserId)`
- `idx_collections_visibility_published` on `(visibility, publishedAt DESC)` — drives browse query

System collections (`systemKind in ('installed','saved')`) keep `visibility='private'` and are excluded from browse. They get a deterministic auto-generated shortcode like any other row; the difference is purely how the UI treats them.

Migration: generate via `bun run db:generate` (creates a new file under `drizzle/`). A small backfill is needed for existing rows — see "Backfill" below.

## Backfill

A one-shot SQL block inside the new migration (so it runs alongside the schema change):

```sql
-- 1. add new columns + enum (drizzle-kit emits this)
-- 2. backfill slugs for existing rows
update collections
set slug = lower(substring(md5(id || random()::text) for 10))
where slug is null;
-- 3. add the NOT NULL + UNIQUE constraints last
alter table collections alter column slug set not null;
alter table collections add constraint collections_slug_unique unique (slug);
```

Collisions during backfill are vanishingly unlikely (~36^10 keyspace, hundreds of rows), but if drizzle-kit's emitted statement order isn't friendly we may need a hand-written migration in `drizzle/`. Verify by running `bun run db:generate` and reading the diff before applying.

## Shared validators

New file: `shared/validators/collection.ts`

Mirrors the style of `shared/validators/profile.ts`:

```ts
export const COLLECTION_NAME_MIN = 1
export const COLLECTION_NAME_MAX = 100
export const COLLECTION_DESC_MAX = 500

export const CollectionVisibility = z.enum(["private", "public"])

export const CreateCollectionInput = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(COLLECTION_NAME_MIN).max(COLLECTION_NAME_MAX),
  nameZh: z.string().max(COLLECTION_NAME_MAX).optional(),
  description: z.string().max(COLLECTION_DESC_MAX).optional(),
  descriptionZh: z.string().max(COLLECTION_DESC_MAX).optional(),
  visibility: CollectionVisibility.default("private"),
})

export const UpdateCollectionInput = CreateCollectionInput.partial()
export const AddItemInput = z.object({ extensionId: z.string().min(1) })
```

Export inferred types alongside (`CreateCollectionInput`, etc.) — same pattern the rest of the repo uses.

## Server: shortcode + queries

### `server/utils/collections/shortcode.ts` (new)

Single helper `generateShortcode(): string` returning 10 base36 chars from `crypto.randomBytes` (Bun-compatible). Routes that insert call a small wrapper `generateUniqueShortcode(db)` which retries up to 5 times on unique-constraint violation before failing — pragmatic, matches the patterns in the existing publish code.

### `server/utils/queries/collections.ts` (new)

Functions (all parameterized by `db = useDb()`):

- `listForOwner(userId)` → `Array<{ id, slug, name, nameZh, description, descriptionZh, visibility, systemKind, itemCount, updatedAt }>` — owner's collections, includes the `saved` system row tagged via `systemKind`, **excludes** `installed`. Item counts via a single grouped subquery on `collection_items`.
- `getBySlug(slug, viewerUserId | null)` → collection + items (paginated) + owner display name. Throws `not_found` when row missing; throws `forbidden` when row is `private` and viewer ≠ owner.
- `listPublicPaged({ page, pageSize })` → public browse list, ordered by `publishedAt DESC`, joined to user for owner display name and aggregate item count.
- `getMembershipForExtension(extensionId, userId)` → `Set<string>` of collection IDs that already contain this extension — feeds the popover checkbox state.
- `create({ ownerUserId, ...input })`, `update(slug, ownerUserId, input)`, `remove(slug, ownerUserId)`, `addItem(slug, ownerUserId, extensionId)`, `removeItem(slug, ownerUserId, extensionId)` — `remove*` reject system collections (`systemKind != null`) with a typed `system_collection_locked` error.

`update` is responsible for setting `publishedAt` on the first private→public transition (and leaving it on the row when toggled back to private — keeps "ever-published" sort stable; can revisit if it matters).

Both `addItem` and `removeItem` also bump `updatedAt` on the parent collection.

A small `CollectionError` class (akin to `InstallError` in `shared/installs/record.ts`) carries `code` strings: `not_found`, `forbidden`, `system_collection_locked`, `slug_taken`, `name_required`. Server handlers map codes → HTTP status.

### `server/utils/queries/profile.ts` (existing — extend)

Add `getCollectionsForUser(userId): Promise<ProfileCollectionRow[]>` returning the same shape as `listForOwner` minus item rows. Wire it into `server/api/internal/profile/section.get.ts` under a new `"collections"` case and into the `SectionKey` union + `SectionResponse`.

## Server: API routes

All under `server/api/internal/collections/`. All use `requireUser(event)` and Zod-validated bodies via `readValidatedBody`. Response shape mirrors the existing `installs.post.ts` convention: `{ ok: true, ...payload }` on success, `createError({ statusCode, statusMessage })` on failure.

| Route | Method | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `index.get.ts` | GET | List the current user's collections (incl. `saved`, excl. `installed`). |
| `index.post.ts` | POST | Create a new collection. Body: `CreateCollectionInput`. Returns `{ ok, slug }`. |
| `[slug].get.ts` | GET | Fetch a single collection. Owner sees private rows; visitors see public only. Includes paginated items. |
| `[slug].patch.ts` | PATCH | Update name / description / visibility. Body: `UpdateCollectionInput`. Owner-only. Rejects system collections. |
| `[slug].delete.ts` | DELETE | Delete a collection. Owner-only. Rejects system collections. |
| `[slug]/items.post.ts` | POST | Add an extension. Body: `AddItemInput`. Owner-only. Idempotent (PK ignore-on-conflict). |
| `[slug]/items/[extensionId].delete.ts` | DELETE | Remove an extension. Owner-only. |
| `public.get.ts` | GET | Paginated public collections for the browse page. Query: `?page=1&pageSize=24`. |
| `for-extension.get.ts` | GET | Membership lookup. Query: `?extensionId=...`. Returns `{ collectionIds: string[] }`. Powers the picker checkboxes. |

Note: `for-extension.get.ts` is intentionally **not** under `[slug]/` — it's a per-extension query keyed by user, not by collection.

Saved collection lazy-init: `getOrCreateSavedCollection(userId)` already exists implicitly inside `recordInstall` (see `shared/installs/record.ts`). Extract it into `server/utils/queries/collections.ts` so `index.get.ts` and the items routes can lazy-create the "Saved" row on first access — keeps existing `recordInstall` behavior intact (it will import the same helper rather than inline-create).

## UI changes

### Bookmark picker — `app/components/extension/SaveToCollectionPopover.vue` (new)

Replaces the inline action in `SaveButton.vue`. Behavior:

1. On first click, fetch `/api/internal/collections` + `/api/internal/collections/for-extension?extensionId=…` in parallel (cached in-memory for the popover's lifetime). Unauthenticated → redirect to `/sign-in` (mirrors current `SaveButton` behavior).
2. Render a popover anchored to the bookmark icon:
   - Pinned row at top: **Saved** (system collection) with a checkbox. Toggling POSTs/DELETEs against `/api/internal/collections/<saved-slug>/items`.
   - List of user-created collections with checkboxes. Each toggle is an immediate POST/DELETE.
   - Footer: an inline "+ New collection" input that creates and adds in one shot (POST `/api/internal/collections`, then POST `/items`).
3. The bookmark icon's filled-state reflects whether the extension is in **any** collection (membership set non-empty).

The popover uses Reka UI's `Popover` (already a project dep through shadcn-vue). Localize labels via `useI18n()`.

### `SaveButton.vue` (existing — replace internals)

Becomes a thin wrapper that opens `SaveToCollectionPopover`. Keeps the same props/markup contract so callers in `ExtHero` don't change. The 3-second "Saved" transient is replaced by the more honest "is it in any collection" indicator.

### Profile section — `app/components/profile/SectionCollections.vue` (new) + `SectionRail.vue` (extend)

Add `"collections"` to the section enum in `SectionRail.vue`, `server/api/internal/profile/section.get.ts`, and `shared/types`. The new component lists user collections in cards: name (bilingual), item count, visibility chip (private/public), updated-at. Each card links to `/collections/<slug>`. Inline actions: rename, delete, toggle visibility, copy public link.

Rename/move existing `SectionSaved.vue`? Keep it for now under a separate `"saved"` tab — many users have built up muscle memory. Mark in a follow-up issue whether to fold it into `SectionCollections.vue` once the new feature lands.

### Pages

- `app/pages/collections/index.vue` (new) — Public browse. Fetches `/api/internal/collections/public`. Grid of `CollectionCard` (new shared component under `app/components/collection/`). Empty state when no public collections exist. Locale-aware via `useLocalePath`. Pagination via query string.
- `app/pages/collections/[slug].vue` (new) — Detail. SSR-fetches the collection. Renders header (name, description, owner, visibility chip), item grid using existing extension-card primitives (reuse from `SectionSaved.vue` or `app/components/extension/ExtensionCard.vue` if it exists), and an owner-only edit/delete/visibility-toggle bar. Toggling visibility makes a PATCH; toggling shows a "Copy link" affordance once public.

### Navigation

Add a "Collections" link to the TopBar (between Extensions and the user menu) pointing at `/collections`. Hidden when logged out? No — collections browse is public.

### i18n

Extend `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json` under existing namespaces:

- `profile.sections.collections` — sidebar label.
- `profile.emptyCollections.{title,body}` — empty state.
- `collections.browse.{title,subtitle,empty}` — `/collections` page copy.
- `collections.detail.{visibilityPrivate,visibilityPublic,owner,itemsHeading,empty,copyLink,linkCopied,confirmDelete}`
- `collections.actions.{create,rename,delete,makePublic,makePrivate,save,cancel}`
- `collections.form.{namePlaceholder,nameZhPlaceholder,descriptionPlaceholder,descriptionZhPlaceholder,visibilityHelp}`
- `common.saveToCollection`, `common.newCollection`, `common.added`, `common.removed`

Chinese translations included in the same commits.

## Files to create

```
shared/db/schema/collection.ts                            # extend
shared/validators/collection.ts                           # new
shared/types.ts                                           # extend (ProfileCollectionRow, CollectionVisibility)
server/utils/collections/shortcode.ts                     # new
server/utils/queries/collections.ts                       # new
server/utils/queries/profile.ts                           # extend (getCollectionsForUser)
server/api/internal/collections/index.get.ts              # new
server/api/internal/collections/index.post.ts             # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug].get.ts             # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug].patch.ts           # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug].delete.ts          # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug]/items.post.ts      # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug]/items/[extensionId].delete.ts  # new
server/api/internal/collections/public.get.ts             # new
server/api/internal/collections/for-extension.get.ts      # new
server/api/internal/profile/section.get.ts                # extend (collections case)
app/pages/collections/index.vue                           # new
app/pages/collections/[slug].vue                          # new
app/components/extension/SaveToCollectionPopover.vue      # new
app/components/extension/SaveButton.vue                   # replace internals, keep props
app/components/collection/CollectionCard.vue              # new
app/components/collection/CollectionForm.vue              # new (used in create/rename modal)
app/components/collection/VisibilityToggle.vue            # new
app/components/profile/SectionCollections.vue             # new
app/components/profile/SectionRail.vue                    # extend
app/components/layout/TopBar.vue                          # extend (nav link)
i18n/locales/en.json                                      # extend
i18n/locales/zh.json                                      # extend
drizzle/<next>_collections_v2.sql                         # generated + backfill
```

## Reuse existing code

- `requireUser(event)` from `server/utils/auth.ts` for all internal routes.
- `useDb()` from `server/utils/db.ts` for the Drizzle handle.
- `recordInstall` orchestration in `shared/installs/record.ts` — extract its inline `getOrCreateSavedCollection` and reuse from the new collections queries. Keep `recordInstall` semantics unchanged.
- `getSavedForUser` in `server/utils/queries/profile.ts` — stays; the new `getCollectionsForUser` is additive.
- `app/components/profile/SectionSaved.vue` row layout — copy the card structure into `CollectionCard.vue` and `SectionCollections.vue`.
- Zod validators in `shared/validators/profile.ts` for shape, naming, and `_MAX` constant style.
- Reka UI `Popover` primitive — already wired through shadcn-vue (`app/components/ui/`) — use for the picker.

## Out of scope (deferred)

- No `/api/v1/collections/*` public registry endpoints. CLI cannot install-by-collection.
- No collection-level ratings, comments, or fork/clone.
- No org-scoped or department-scoped collections (only user-owned). Multi-tenancy hook exists on `collections.ownerUserId`; org ownership can be added later by introducing `ownerOrgId`.
- No featured/curated collections on the home page. The home featured hero stays as-is.
- No `unlisted` third visibility state — can be added later by extending the enum without breaking rows.

## Verification

End-to-end manual flow (run `bun run dev`, sign in as a test user):

1. **Schema** — `bun run db:generate` produces a clean migration; `bun run db:migrate` applies it; existing `saved` rows now have non-null `slug`, `visibility='private'`, item counts unchanged.
2. **Bookmark picker** — Open any extension detail page. Click bookmark → popover shows "Saved" pinned at top. Tick → row appears in Profile → Saved. Untick → row disappears.
3. **Create collection** — In the popover, "+ New collection" → name "Test Pack" → it gains a checkbox, ticking adds the current extension.
4. **Profile section** — Profile → Collections shows "Test Pack" (1 item) and "Saved" tagged as system.
5. **Detail page** — Click "Test Pack" card → lands on `/collections/<shortcode>` with the extension listed. Owner-only edit bar visible.
6. **Visibility toggle** — Toggle Test Pack to Public. URL stays the same. `publishedAt` populated. Open in a private window (signed out) → page loads, no edit bar, item visible.
7. **Browse** — Sign out, visit `/collections` → "Test Pack" appears in the public browse grid with owner name.
8. **Delete** — Back as owner, delete Test Pack → confirms, removes from profile + browse. Confirm `collection_items` cascade fired (no orphans).
9. **System collection guard** — Try `DELETE /api/internal/collections/<saved-slug>` directly via devtools → 422 with `system_collection_locked`.
10. **i18n** — Switch locale via TopBar. Every new string renders in zh; nothing falls back to a missing-key marker.

Automated coverage (added in the same PR series):

- `shared/validators/collection.test.ts` — Zod input cases (length bounds, visibility enum).
- `server/utils/queries/collections.test.ts` — integration test against the test DB: create + add + visibility toggle + browse + delete + system-collection guard.
- `shared/installs/record.test.ts` — keep green; confirms the extract-and-reuse of `getOrCreateSavedCollection` didn't regress install events.

Phase/commit slicing (per `docs/plan.md` §13):

1. `feat(p2-db): extend collections schema with slug + visibility + description` — schema + migration + backfill.
2. `feat(p9-collections): collections query layer + shortcode helper` — `server/utils/queries/collections.ts`, `shortcode.ts`, extract `getOrCreateSavedCollection`.
3. `feat(p9-collections): internal collections API routes` — all `server/api/internal/collections/*` routes + validators.
4. `feat(p9-collections): save-to-collection popover + SaveButton rewire` — UI for picker + popover.
5. `feat(p9-collections): /collections browse + detail pages` — public-facing pages + nav link.
6. `feat(p9-collections): profile collections section` — `SectionCollections` + section.get.ts wiring.
7. `feat(i18n): collections strings (en + zh)` — locale bundles.

Each step ends green on the validate pipeline before the next begins.

input.plan:
# Collections feature — design & implementation plan

## Context

Users currently have one implicit bookmark bucket — the `systemKind='saved'` collection — exposed via the bookmark button on extension detail pages and surfaced in Profile → "Saved". The schema (`shared/db/schema/collection.ts`) and `docs/plan.md` already anticipate user-created collections (P0 page `/[locale]/collections`, P1 detail page `/[locale]/collections/[id]`), but the feature was never wired up. This change finishes the design:

- Lets users create named collections (with optional bilingual name + description), add/remove extensions, and toggle each collection between **private** and **public**.
- Public collections get a shareable, stable, copy-pasteable URL (`/collections/<shortcode>`) and appear on a `/collections` browse page.
- The existing bookmark button becomes a small picker so one-tap "Save" still works while explicit named-collection placement is one extra click away.
- Web scope only this round. The `/api/v1/` public registry contract is **not** touched — CLI install-collection support is deferred so the UX can shake out first.

Outcome: users have a real "save and organize" surface in the marketplace, and can share curated bundles by URL.

## Pivotal decisions (taken with the user)

1. **Bookmark UX** — Click opens a small popover with a checkbox list of the user's collections + "New collection…" inline. The system **Saved** collection is pinned at the top of the list so one-tap save still works.
2. **Visibility model** — Two-state enum: `private` | `public`. A `/collections` browse page lists public collections; direct link works for both.
3. **API scope** — Internal routes under `server/api/internal/collections/...` only. No `/api/v1/` collection endpoints, no new CLI command. Defer.
4. **URL shape** — `/collections/<shortcode>` where `shortcode` is a 10-character base36 string generated on insert. Same path for private (owner-only access) and public.

## Schema changes

File: `shared/db/schema/collection.ts`

Add columns to `collections`:

| Column | Type | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `slug` | `text not null unique` | 10-char base36 shortcode, generated server-side |
| `description` | `text` (nullable) | English description, max 500 |
| `descriptionZh` | `text` (nullable) | Chinese description, max 500 |
| `visibility` | `collection_visibility` enum (`private`, `public`) `not null default 'private'` | New enum |
| `publishedAt` | `timestamp({ withTimezone: true })` (nullable) | Set the first time `visibility` flips to `public`; used for browse sort |
| `updatedAt` | `timestamp({ withTimezone: true }) not null default now()` | Bumped on item add/remove or metadata edit; matches the pattern in `extensions` |

New enum at top of file: `export const collectionVisibilityEnum = pgEnum("collection_visibility", ["private", "public"])`.

Indexes:

- `idx_collections_owner_user` on `(ownerUserId)`
- `idx_collections_visibility_published` on `(visibility, publishedAt DESC)` — drives browse query

System collections (`systemKind in ('installed','saved')`) keep `visibility='private'` and are excluded from browse. They get a deterministic auto-generated shortcode like any other row; the difference is purely how the UI treats them.

Migration: generate via `bun run db:generate` (creates a new file under `drizzle/`). A small backfill is needed for existing rows — see "Backfill" below.

## Backfill

A one-shot SQL block inside the new migration (so it runs alongside the schema change):

```sql
-- 1. add new columns + enum (drizzle-kit emits this)
-- 2. backfill slugs for existing rows
update collections
set slug = lower(substring(md5(id || random()::text) for 10))
where slug is null;
-- 3. add the NOT NULL + UNIQUE constraints last
alter table collections alter column slug set not null;
alter table collections add constraint collections_slug_unique unique (slug);
```

Collisions during backfill are vanishingly unlikely (~36^10 keyspace, hundreds of rows), but if drizzle-kit's emitted statement order isn't friendly we may need a hand-written migration in `drizzle/`. Verify by running `bun run db:generate` and reading the diff before applying.

## Shared validators

New file: `shared/validators/collection.ts`

Mirrors the style of `shared/validators/profile.ts`:

```ts
export const COLLECTION_NAME_MIN = 1
export const COLLECTION_NAME_MAX = 100
export const COLLECTION_DESC_MAX = 500

  name: z.string().min(COLLECTION_NAME_MIN).max(COLLECTION_NAME_MAX),

export const UpdateCollectionInput = CreateCollectionInput.partial()

Routes that insert call a small wrapper `generateUniqueShortcode(db)` which retries up to 5 times on unique-constraint violation before failing — pragmatic, matches the patterns in the existing publish code.

   - List of user-created collections with checkboxes. Each toggle is an immediate POST/DELETE.

### `SaveButton.vue` (existing — replace internals)

Becomes a thin wrapper that opens `SaveToCollectionPopover`. Keeps the same props/markup contract so callers in `ExtHero` don't change. The 3-second "Saved" transient is replaced by the more honest "is it in any collection" indicator.

### Profile section — `app/components/profile/SectionCollections.vue` (new) + `SectionRail.vue` (extend)

Add `"collections"` to the section enum in `SectionRail.vue`, `server/api/internal/profile/section.get.ts`, and `shared/types`. The new component lists user collections in cards: name (bilingual), item count, visibility chip (private/public), updated-at. Each card links to `/collections/<slug>`. Inline actions: rename, delete, toggle visibility, copy public link.

Rename/move existing `SectionSaved.vue`? Keep it for now under a separate `"saved"` tab — many users have built up muscle memory. Mark in a follow-up issue whether to fold it into `SectionCollections.vue` once the new feature lands.

### Pages

- `app/pages/collections/index.vue` (new) — Public browse. Fetches `/api/internal/collections/public`. Grid of `CollectionCard` (new shared component under `app/components/collection/`). Empty state when no public collections exist. Locale-aware via `useLocalePath`. Pagination via query string.
- `app/pages/collections/[slug].vue` (new) — Detail. SSR-fetches the collection. Renders header (name, description, owner, visibility chip), item grid using existing extension-card primitives (reuse from `SectionSaved.vue` or `app/components/extension/ExtensionCard.vue` if it exists), and an owner-only edit/delete/visibility-toggle bar. Toggling visibility makes a PATCH; toggling shows a "Copy link" affordance once public.

### Navigation

Add a "Collections" link to the TopBar (between Extensions and the user menu) pointing at `/collections`. Hidden when logged out? No — collections browse is public.

### i18n

Extend `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json` under existing namespaces:

- `profile.sections.collections` — sidebar label.
- `profile.emptyCollections.{title,body}` — empty state.
- `collections.browse.{title,subtitle,empty}` — `/collections` page copy.
- `collections.detail.{visibilityPrivate,visibilityPublic,owner,itemsHeading,empty,copyLink,linkCopied,confirmDelete}`
- `collections.actions.{create,rename,delete,makePublic,makePrivate,save,cancel}`
- `collections.form.{namePlaceholder,nameZhPlaceholder,descriptionPlaceholder,descriptionZhPlaceholder,visibilityHelp}`
- `common.saveToCollection`, `common.newCollection`, `common.added`, `common.removed`

Chinese translations included in the same commits.

## Files to create

```
shared/db/schema/collection.ts                            # extend
shared/validators/collection.ts                           # new
shared/types.ts                                           # extend (ProfileCollectionRow, CollectionVisibility)
server/utils/collections/shortcode.ts                     # new
server/utils/queries/collections.ts                       # new
server/utils/queries/profile.ts                           # extend (getCollectionsForUser)
server/api/internal/collections/index.get.ts              # new
server/api/internal/collections/index.post.ts             # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug].get.ts             # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug].patch.ts           # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug].delete.ts          # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug]/items.post.ts      # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug]/items/[extensionId].delete.ts  # new
server/api/internal/collections/public.get.ts             # new
server/api/internal/collections/for-extension.get.ts      # new
server/api/internal/profile/section.get.ts                # extend (collections case)
app/pages/collections/index.vue                           # new
app/pages/collections/[slug].vue                          # new
app/components/extension/SaveToCollectionPopover.vue      # new
app/components/extension/SaveButton.vue                   # replace internals, keep props
app/components/collection/CollectionCard.vue              # new
app/components/collection/CollectionForm.vue              # new (used in create/rename modal)
app/components/collection/VisibilityToggle.vue            # new
app/components/profile/SectionCollections.vue             # new
app/components/profile/SectionRail.vue                    # extend
app/components/layout/TopBar.vue                          # extend (nav link)
i18n/locales/en.json                                      # extend
i18n/locales/zh.json                                      # extend
drizzle/<next>_collections_v2.sql                         # generated + backfill
```

## Reuse existing code

- `requireUser(event)` from `server/utils/auth.ts` for all internal routes.
- `useDb()` from `server/utils/db.ts` for the Drizzle handle.
- `recordInstall` orchestration in `shared/installs/record.ts` — extract its inline `getOrCreateSavedCollection` and reuse from the new collections queries. Keep `recordInstall` semantics unchanged.
- `getSavedForUser` in `server/utils/queries/profile.ts` — stays; the new `getCollectionsForUser` is additive.
- `app/components/profile/SectionSaved.vue` row layout — copy the card structure into `CollectionCard.vue` and `SectionCollections.vue`.
- Zod validators in `shared/validators/profile.ts` for shape, naming, and `_MAX` constant style.
- Reka UI `Popover` primitive — already wired through shadcn-vue (`app/components/ui/`) — use for the picker.

## Out of scope (deferred)

- No `/api/v1/collections/*` public registry endpoints. CLI cannot install-by-collection.
- No collection-level ratings, comments, or fork/clone.
- No org-scoped or department-scoped collections (only user-owned). Multi-tenancy hook exists on `collections.ownerUserId`; org ownership can be added later by introducing `ownerOrgId`.
- No featured/curated collections on the home page. The home featured hero stays as-is.
- No `unlisted` third visibility state — can be added later by extending the enum without breaking rows.

## Verification

End-to-end manual flow (run `bun run dev`, sign in as a test user):

1. **Schema** — `bun run db:generate` produces a clean migration; `bun run db:migrate` applies it; existing `saved` rows now have non-null `slug`, `visibility='private'`, item counts unchanged.
2. **Bookmark picker** — Open any extension detail page. Click bookmark → popover shows "Saved" pinned at top. Tick → row appears in Profile → Saved. Untick → row disappears.
3. **Create collection** — In the popover, "+ New collection" → name "Test Pack" → it gains a checkbox, ticking adds the current extension.
4. **Profile section** — Profile → Collections shows "Test Pack" (1 item) and "Saved" tagged as system.
5. **Detail page** — Click "Test Pack" card → lands on `/collections/<shortcode>` with the extension listed. Owner-only edit bar visible.
6. **Visibility toggle** — Toggle Test Pack to Public. URL stays the same. `publishedAt` populated. Open in a private window (signed out) → page loads, no edit bar, item visible.
7. **Browse** — Sign out, visit `/collections` → "Test Pack" appears in the public browse grid with owner name.
8. **Delete** — Back as owner, delete Test Pack → confirms, removes from profile + browse. Confirm `collection_items` cascade fired (no orphans).
9. **System collection guard** — Try `DELETE /api/internal/collections/<saved-slug>` directly via devtools → 422 with `system_collection_locked`.
10. **i18n** — Switch locale via TopBar. Every new string renders in zh; nothing falls back to a missing-key marker.

Automated coverage (added in the same PR series):

- `shared/validators/collection.test.ts` — Zod input cases (length bounds, visibility enum).
- `server/utils/queries/collections.test.ts` — integration test against the test DB: create + add + visibility toggle + browse + delete + system-collection guard.
- `shared/installs/record.test.ts` — keep green; confirms the extract-and-reuse of `getOrCreateSavedCollection` didn't regress install events.

Phase/commit slicing (per `docs/plan.md` §13):

1. `feat(p2-db): extend collections schema with slug + visibility + description` — schema + migration + backfill.
2. `feat(p9-collections): collections query layer + shortcode helper` — `server/utils/queries/collections.ts`, `shortcode.ts`, extract `getOrCreateSavedCollection`.
3. `feat(p9-collections): internal collections API routes` — all `server/api/internal/collections/*` routes + validators.
4. `feat(p9-collections): save-to-collection popover + SaveButton rewire` — UI for picker + popover.
5. `feat(p9-collections): /collections browse + detail pages` — public-facing pages + nav link.
6. `feat(p9-collections): profile collections section` — `SectionCollections` + section.get.ts wiring.
7. `feat(i18n): collections strings (en + zh)` — locale bundles.

Each step ends green on the validate pipeline before the next begins.

## Approved Plan:
# Collections feature — design & implementation plan

## Context

Users currently have one implicit bookmark bucket — the `systemKind='saved'` collection — exposed via the bookmark button on extension detail pages and surfaced in Profile → "Saved". The schema (`shared/db/schema/collection.ts`) and `docs/plan.md` already anticipate user-created collections (P0 page `/[locale]/collections`, P1 detail page `/[locale]/collections/[id]`), but the feature was never wired up. This change finishes the design:

- Lets users create named collections (with optional bilingual name + description), add/remove extensions, and toggle each collection between **private** and **public**.
- Public collections get a shareable, stable, copy-pasteable URL (`/collections/<shortcode>`) and appear on a `/collections` browse page.
- The existing bookmark button becomes a small picker so one-tap "Save" still works while explicit named-collection placement is one extra click away.
- Web scope only this round. The `/api/v1/` public registry contract is **not** touched — CLI install-collection support is deferred so the UX can shake out first.

Outcome: users have a real "save and organize" surface in the marketplace, and can share curated bundles by URL.

## Pivotal decisions (taken with the user)

1. **Bookmark UX** — Click opens a small popover with a checkbox list of the user's collections + "New collection…" inline. The system **Saved** collection is pinned at the top of the list so one-tap save still works.
2. **Visibility model** — Two-state enum: `private` | `public`. A `/collections` browse page lists public collections; direct link works for both.
3. **API scope** — Internal routes under `server/api/internal/collections/...` only. No `/api/v1/` collection endpoints, no new CLI command. Defer.
4. **URL shape** — `/collections/<shortcode>` where `shortcode` is a 10-character base36 string generated on insert. Same path for private (owner-only access) and public.

## Schema changes

File: `shared/db/schema/collection.ts`

Add columns to `collections`:

| Column | Type | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `slug` | `text not null unique` | 10-char base36 shortcode, generated server-side |
| `description` | `text` (nullable) | English description, max 500 |
| `descriptionZh` | `text` (nullable) | Chinese description, max 500 |
| `visibility` | `collection_visibility` enum (`private`, `public`) `not null default 'private'` | New enum |
| `publishedAt` | `timestamp({ withTimezone: true })` (nullable) | Set the first time `visibility` flips to `public`; used for browse sort |
| `updatedAt` | `timestamp({ withTimezone: true }) not null default now()` | Bumped on item add/remove or metadata edit; matches the pattern in `extensions` |

New enum at top of file: `export const collectionVisibilityEnum = pgEnum("collection_visibility", ["private", "public"])`.

Indexes:

- `idx_collections_owner_user` on `(ownerUserId)`
- `idx_collections_visibility_published` on `(visibility, publishedAt DESC)` — drives browse query

System collections (`systemKind in ('installed','saved')`) keep `visibility='private'` and are excluded from browse. They get a deterministic auto-generated shortcode like any other row; the difference is purely how the UI treats them.

Migration: generate via `bun run db:generate` (creates a new file under `drizzle/`). A small backfill is needed for existing rows — see "Backfill" below.

## Backfill

A one-shot SQL block inside the new migration (so it runs alongside the schema change):

```sql
-- 1. add new columns + enum (drizzle-kit emits this)
-- 2. backfill slugs for existing rows
update collections
set slug = lower(substring(md5(id || random()::text) for 10))
where slug is null;
-- 3. add the NOT NULL + UNIQUE constraints last
alter table collections alter column slug set not null;
alter table collections add constraint collections_slug_unique unique (slug);
```

Collisions during backfill are vanishingly unlikely (~36^10 keyspace, hundreds of rows), but if drizzle-kit's emitted statement order isn't friendly we may need a hand-written migration in `drizzle/`. Verify by running `bun run db:generate` and reading the diff before applying.

## Shared validators

New file: `shared/validators/collection.ts`

Mirrors the style of `shared/validators/profile.ts`:

```ts
export const COLLECTION_NAME_MIN = 1
export const COLLECTION_NAME_MAX = 100
export const COLLECTION_DESC_MAX = 500

export const CollectionVisibility = z.enum(["private", "public"])

export const CreateCollectionInput = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(COLLECTION_NAME_MIN).max(COLLECTION_NAME_MAX),
  nameZh: z.string().max(COLLECTION_NAME_MAX).optional(),
  description: z.string().max(COLLECTION_DESC_MAX).optional(),
  descriptionZh: z.string().max(COLLECTION_DESC_MAX).optional(),
  visibility: CollectionVisibility.default("private"),
})

export const UpdateCollectionInput = CreateCollectionInput.partial()
export const AddItemInput = z.object({ extensionId: z.string().min(1) })
```

Export inferred types alongside (`CreateCollectionInput`, etc.) — same pattern the rest of the repo uses.

## Server: shortcode + queries

### `server/utils/collections/shortcode.ts` (new)

Single helper `generateShortcode(): string` returning 10 base36 chars from `crypto.randomBytes` (Bun-compatible). Routes that insert call a small wrapper `generateUniqueShortcode(db)` which retries up to 5 times on unique-constraint violation before failing — pragmatic, matches the patterns in the existing publish code.

### `server/utils/queries/collections.ts` (new)

Functions (all parameterized by `db = useDb()`):

- `listForOwner(userId)` → `Array<{ id, slug, name, nameZh, description, descriptionZh, visibility, systemKind, itemCount, updatedAt }>` — owner's collections, includes the `saved` system row tagged via `systemKind`, **excludes** `installed`. Item counts via a single grouped subquery on `collection_items`.
- `getBySlug(slug, viewerUserId | null)` → collection + items (paginated) + owner display name. Throws `not_found` when row missing; throws `forbidden` when row is `private` and viewer ≠ owner.
- `listPublicPaged({ page, pageSize })` → public browse list, ordered by `publishedAt DESC`, joined to user for owner display name and aggregate item count.
- `getMembershipForExtension(extensionId, userId)` → `Set<string>` of collection IDs that already contain this extension — feeds the popover checkbox state.
- `create({ ownerUserId, ...input })`, `update(slug, ownerUserId, input)`, `remove(slug, ownerUserId)`, `addItem(slug, ownerUserId, extensionId)`, `removeItem(slug, ownerUserId, extensionId)` — `remove*` reject system collections (`systemKind != null`) with a typed `system_collection_locked` error.

`update` is responsible for setting `publishedAt` on the first private→public transition (and leaving it on the row when toggled back to private — keeps "ever-published" sort stable; can revisit if it matters).

Both `addItem` and `removeItem` also bump `updatedAt` on the parent collection.

A small `CollectionError` class (akin to `InstallError` in `shared/installs/record.ts`) carries `code` strings: `not_found`, `forbidden`, `system_collection_locked`, `slug_taken`, `name_required`. Server handlers map codes → HTTP status.

### `server/utils/queries/profile.ts` (existing — extend)

Add `getCollectionsForUser(userId): Promise<ProfileCollectionRow[]>` returning the same shape as `listForOwner` minus item rows. Wire it into `server/api/internal/profile/section.get.ts` under a new `"collections"` case and into the `SectionKey` union + `SectionResponse`.

## Server: API routes

All under `server/api/internal/collections/`. All use `requireUser(event)` and Zod-validated bodies via `readValidatedBody`. Response shape mirrors the existing `installs.post.ts` convention: `{ ok: true, ...payload }` on success, `createError({ statusCode, statusMessage })` on failure.

| Route | Method | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `index.get.ts` | GET | List the current user's collections (incl. `saved`, excl. `installed`). |
| `index.post.ts` | POST | Create a new collection. Body: `CreateCollectionInput`. Returns `{ ok, slug }`. |
| `[slug].get.ts` | GET | Fetch a single collection. Owner sees private rows; visitors see public only. Includes paginated items. |
| `[slug].patch.ts` | PATCH | Update name / description / visibility. Body: `UpdateCollectionInput`. Owner-only. Rejects system collections. |
| `[slug].delete.ts` | DELETE | Delete a collection. Owner-only. Rejects system collections. |
| `[slug]/items.post.ts` | POST | Add an extension. Body: `AddItemInput`. Owner-only. Idempotent (PK ignore-on-conflict). |
| `[slug]/items/[extensionId].delete.ts` | DELETE | Remove an extension. Owner-only. |
| `public.get.ts` | GET | Paginated public collections for the browse page. Query: `?page=1&pageSize=24`. |
| `for-extension.get.ts` | GET | Membership lookup. Query: `?extensionId=...`. Returns `{ collectionIds: string[] }`. Powers the picker checkboxes. |

Note: `for-extension.get.ts` is intentionally **not** under `[slug]/` — it's a per-extension query keyed by user, not by collection.

Saved collection lazy-init: `getOrCreateSavedCollection(userId)` already exists implicitly inside `recordInstall` (see `shared/installs/record.ts`). Extract it into `server/utils/queries/collections.ts` so `index.get.ts` and the items routes can lazy-create the "Saved" row on first access — keeps existing `recordInstall` behavior intact (it will import the same helper rather than inline-create).

## UI changes

### Bookmark picker — `app/components/extension/SaveToCollectionPopover.vue` (new)

Replaces the inline action in `SaveButton.vue`. Behavior:

1. On first click, fetch `/api/internal/collections` + `/api/internal/collections/for-extension?extensionId=…` in parallel (cached in-memory for the popover's lifetime). Unauthenticated → redirect to `/sign-in` (mirrors current `SaveButton` behavior).
2. Render a popover anchored to the bookmark icon:
   - Pinned row at top: **Saved** (system collection) with a checkbox. Toggling POSTs/DELETEs against `/api/internal/collections/<saved-slug>/items`.
   - List of user-created collections with checkboxes. Each toggle is an immediate POST/DELETE.
   - Footer: an inline "+ New collection" input that creates and adds in one shot (POST `/api/internal/collections`, then POST `/items`).
3. The bookmark icon's filled-state reflects whether the extension is in **any** collection (membership set non-empty).

The popover uses Reka UI's `Popover` (already a project dep through shadcn-vue). Localize labels via `useI18n()`.

### `SaveButton.vue` (existing — replace internals)

Becomes a thin wrapper that opens `SaveToCollectionPopover`. Keeps the same props/markup contract so callers in `ExtHero` don't change. The 3-second "Saved" transient is replaced by the more honest "is it in any collection" indicator.

### Profile section — `app/components/profile/SectionCollections.vue` (new) + `SectionRail.vue` (extend)

Add `"collections"` to the section enum in `SectionRail.vue`, `server/api/internal/profile/section.get.ts`, and `shared/types`. The new component lists user collections in cards: name (bilingual), item count, visibility chip (private/public), updated-at. Each card links to `/collections/<slug>`. Inline actions: rename, delete, toggle visibility, copy public link.

Rename/move existing `SectionSaved.vue`? Keep it for now under a separate `"saved"` tab — many users have built up muscle memory. Mark in a follow-up issue whether to fold it into `SectionCollections.vue` once the new feature lands.

### Pages

- `app/pages/collections/index.vue` (new) — Public browse. Fetches `/api/internal/collections/public`. Grid of `CollectionCard` (new shared component under `app/components/collection/`). Empty state when no public collections exist. Locale-aware via `useLocalePath`. Pagination via query string.
- `app/pages/collections/[slug].vue` (new) — Detail. SSR-fetches the collection. Renders header (name, description, owner, visibility chip), item grid using existing extension-card primitives (reuse from `SectionSaved.vue` or `app/components/extension/ExtensionCard.vue` if it exists), and an owner-only edit/delete/visibility-toggle bar. Toggling visibility makes a PATCH; toggling shows a "Copy link" affordance once public.

### Navigation

Add a "Collections" link to the TopBar (between Extensions and the user menu) pointing at `/collections`. Hidden when logged out? No — collections browse is public.

### i18n

Extend `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json` under existing namespaces:

- `profile.sections.collections` — sidebar label.
- `profile.emptyCollections.{title,body}` — empty state.
- `collections.browse.{title,subtitle,empty}` — `/collections` page copy.
- `collections.detail.{visibilityPrivate,visibilityPublic,owner,itemsHeading,empty,copyLink,linkCopied,confirmDelete}`
- `collections.actions.{create,rename,delete,makePublic,makePrivate,save,cancel}`
- `collections.form.{namePlaceholder,nameZhPlaceholder,descriptionPlaceholder,descriptionZhPlaceholder,visibilityHelp}`
- `common.saveToCollection`, `common.newCollection`, `common.added`, `common.removed`

Chinese translations included in the same commits.

## Files to create

```
shared/db/schema/collection.ts                            # extend
shared/validators/collection.ts                           # new
shared/types.ts                                           # extend (ProfileCollectionRow, CollectionVisibility)
server/utils/collections/shortcode.ts                     # new
server/utils/queries/collections.ts                       # new
server/utils/queries/profile.ts                           # extend (getCollectionsForUser)
server/api/internal/collections/index.get.ts              # new
server/api/internal/collections/index.post.ts             # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug].get.ts             # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug].patch.ts           # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug].delete.ts          # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug]/items.post.ts      # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug]/items/[extensionId].delete.ts  # new
server/api/internal/collections/public.get.ts             # new
server/api/internal/collections/for-extension.get.ts      # new

# Collections feature — design & implementation plan

## Context

Users currently have one implicit bookmark bucket — the `systemKind='saved'` collection — exposed via the bookmark button on extension detail pages and surfaced in Profile → "Saved". The schema (`shared/db/schema/collection.ts`) and `docs/plan.md` already anticipate user-created collections (P0 page `/[locale]/collections`, P1 detail page `/[locale]/collections/[id]`), but the feature was never wired up. This change finishes the design:

- Lets users create named collections (with optional bilingual name + description), add/remove extensions, and toggle each collection between **private** and **public**.
- Public collections get a shareable, stable, copy-pasteable URL (`/collections/<shortcode>`) and appear on a `/collections` browse page.
- The existing bookmark button becomes a small picker so one-tap "Save" still works while explicit named-collection placement is one extra click away.
- Web scope only this round. The `/api/v1/` public registry contract is **not** touched — CLI install-collection support is deferred so the UX can shake out first.

Outcome: users have a real "save and organize" surface in the marketplace, and can share curated bundles by URL.

## Pivotal decisions (taken with the user)

1. **Bookmark UX** — Click opens a small popover with a checkbox list of the user's collections + "New collection…" inline. The system **Saved** collection is pinned at the top of the list so one-tap save still works.
2. **Visibility model** — Two-state enum: `private` | `public`. A `/collections` browse page lists public collections; direct link works for both.
3. **API scope** — Internal routes under `server/api/internal/collections/...` only. No `/api/v1/` collection endpoints, no new CLI command. Defer.
4. **URL shape** — `/collections/<shortcode>` where `shortcode` is a 10-character base36 string generated on insert. Same path for private (owner-only access) and public.

## Schema changes

File: `shared/db/schema/collection.ts`

Add columns to `collections`:

| Column | Type | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `slug` | `text not null unique` | 10-char base36 shortcode, generated server-side |
| `description` | `text` (nullable) | English description, max 500 |
| `descriptionZh` | `text` (nullable) | Chinese description, max 500 |
| `visibility` | `collection_visibility` enum (`private`, `public`) `not null default 'private'` | New enum |
| `publishedAt` | `timestamp({ withTimezone: true })` (nullable) | Set the first time `visibility` flips to `public`; used for browse sort |
| `updatedAt` | `timestamp({ withTimezone: true }) not null default now()` | Bumped on item add/remove or metadata edit; matches the pattern in `extensions` |

New enum at top of file: `export const collectionVisibilityEnum = pgEnum("collection_visibility", ["private", "public"])`.

Indexes:

- `idx_collections_owner_user` on `(ownerUserId)`
- `idx_collections_visibility_published` on `(visibility, publishedAt DESC)` — drives browse query

System collections (`systemKind in ('installed','saved')`) keep `visibility='private'` and are excluded from browse. They get a deterministic auto-generated shortcode like any other row; the difference is purely how the UI treats them.

Migration: generate via `bun run db:generate` (creates a new file under `drizzle/`). A small backfill is needed for existing rows — see "Backfill" below.

## Backfill

A one-shot SQL block inside the new migration (so it runs alongside the schema change):

```sql
-- 1. add new columns + enum (drizzle-kit emits this)
-- 2. backfill slugs for existing rows
update collections
set slug = lower(substring(md5(id || random()::text) for 10))
where slug is null;
-- 3. add the NOT NULL + UNIQUE constraints last
alter table collections alter column slug set not null;
alter table collections add constraint collections_slug_unique unique (slug);
```

Collisions during backfill are vanishingly unlikely (~36^10 keyspace, hundreds of rows), but if drizzle-kit's emitted statement order isn't friendly we may need a hand-written migration in `drizzle/`. Verify by running `bun run db:generate` and reading the diff before applying.

## Shared validators

New file: `shared/validators/collection.ts`

Mirrors the style of `shared/validators/profile.ts`:

```ts
export const COLLECTION_NAME_MIN = 1
export const COLLECTION_NAME_MAX = 100
export const COLLECTION_DESC_MAX = 500

export const CollectionVisibility = z.enum(["private", "public"])

export const CreateCollectionInput = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(COLLECTION_NAME_MIN).max(COLLECTION_NAME_MAX),
  nameZh: z.string().max(COLLECTION_NAME_MAX).optional(),
  description: z.string().max(COLLECTION_DESC_MAX).optional(),
  descriptionZh: z.string().max(COLLECTION_DESC_MAX).optional(),
  visibility: CollectionVisibility.default("private"),
})

export const UpdateCollectionInput = CreateCollectionInput.partial()
export const AddItemInput = z.object({ extensionId: z.string().min(1) })
```

Export inferred types alongside (`CreateCollectionInput`, etc.) — same pattern the rest of the repo uses.

## Server: shortcode + queries

### `server/utils/collections/shortcode.ts` (new)

Single helper `generateShortcode(): string` returning 10 base36 chars from `crypto.randomBytes` (Bun-compatible). Routes that insert call a small wrapper `generateUniqueShortcode(db)` which retries up to 5 times on unique-constraint violation before failing — pragmatic, matches the patterns in the existing publish code.

### `server/utils/queries/collections.ts` (new)

Functions (all parameterized by `db = useDb()`):

- `listForOwner(userId)` → `Array<{ id, slug, name, nameZh, description, descriptionZh, visibility, systemKind, itemCount, updatedAt }>` — owner's collections, includes the `saved` system row tagged via `systemKind`, **excludes** `installed`. Item counts via a single grouped subquery on `collection_items`.
- `getBySlug(slug, viewerUserId | null)` → collection + items (paginated) + owner display name. Throws `not_found` when row missing; throws `forbidden` when row is `private` and viewer ≠ owner.
- `listPublicPaged({ page, pageSize })` → public browse list, ordered by `publishedAt DESC`, joined to user for owner display name and aggregate item count.
- `getMembershipForExtension(extensionId, userId)` → `Set<string>` of collection IDs that already contain this extension — feeds the popover checkbox state.
- `create({ ownerUserId, ...input })`, `update(slug, ownerUserId, input)`, `remove(slug, ownerUserId)`, `addItem(slug, ownerUserId, extensionId)`, `removeItem(slug, ownerUserId, extensionId)` — `remove*` reject system collections (`systemKind != null`) with a typed `system_collection_locked` error.

`update` is responsible for setting `publishedAt` on the first private→public transition (and leaving it on the row when toggled back to private — keeps "ever-published" sort stable; can revisit if it matters).

Both `addItem` and `removeItem` also bump `updatedAt` on the parent collection.

A small `CollectionError` class (akin to `InstallError` in `shared/installs/record.ts`) carries `code` strings: `not_found`, `forbidden`, `system_collection_locked`, `slug_taken`, `name_required`. Server handlers map codes → HTTP status.

### `server/utils/queries/profile.ts` (existing — extend)

Add `getCollectionsForUser(userId): Promise<ProfileCollectionRow[]>` returning the same shape as `listForOwner` minus item rows. Wire it into `server/api/internal/profile/section.get.ts` under a new `"collections"` case and into the `SectionKey` union + `SectionResponse`.

## Server: API routes

All under `server/api/internal/collections/`. All use `requireUser(event)` and Zod-validated bodies via `readValidatedBody`. Response shape mirrors the existing `installs.post.ts` convention: `{ ok: true, ...payload }` on success, `createError({ statusCode, statusMessage })` on failure.

| Route | Method | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `index.get.ts` | GET | List the current user's collections (incl. `saved`, excl. `installed`). |
| `index.post.ts` | POST | Create a new collection. Body: `CreateCollectionInput`. Returns `{ ok, slug }`. |
| `[slug].get.ts` | GET | Fetch a single collection. Owner sees private rows; visitors see public only. Includes paginated items. |
| `[slug].patch.ts` | PATCH | Update name / description / visibility. Body: `UpdateCollectionInput`. Owner-only. Rejects system collections. |
| `[slug].delete.ts` | DELETE | Delete a collection. Owner-only. Rejects system collections. |
| `[slug]/items.post.ts` | POST | Add an extension. Body: `AddItemInput`. Owner-only. Idempotent (PK ignore-on-conflict). |
| `[slug]/items/[extensionId].delete.ts` | DELETE | Remove an extension. Owner-only. |
| `public.get.ts` | GET | Paginated public collections for the browse page. Query: `?page=1&pageSize=24`. |
| `for-extension.get.ts` | GET | Membership lookup. Query: `?extensionId=...`. Returns `{ collectionIds: string[] }`. Powers the picker checkboxes. |

Note: `for-extension.get.ts` is intentionally **not** under `[slug]/` — it's a per-extension query keyed by user, not by collection.

Saved collection lazy-init: `getOrCreateSavedCollection(userId)` already exists implicitly inside `recordInstall` (see `shared/installs/record.ts`). Extract it into `server/utils/queries/collections.ts` so `index.get.ts` and the items routes can lazy-create the "Saved" row on first access — keeps existing `recordInstall` behavior intact (it will import the same helper rather than inline-create).

## UI changes

### Bookmark picker — `app/components/extension/SaveToCollectionPopover.vue` (new)

Replaces the inline action in `SaveButton.vue`. Behavior:

1. On first click, fetch `/api/internal/collections` + `/api/internal/collections/for-extension?extensionId=…` in parallel (cached in-memory for the popover's lifetime). Unauthenticated → redirect to `/sign-in` (mirrors current `SaveButton` behavior).
2. Render a popover anchored to the bookmark icon:
   - Pinned row at top: **Saved** (system collection) with a checkbox. Toggling POSTs/DELETEs against `/api/internal/collections/<saved-slug>/items`.
   - List of user-created collections with checkboxes. Each toggle is an immediate POST/DELETE.
   - Footer: an inline "+ New collection" input that creates and adds in one shot (POST `/api/internal/collections`, then POST `/items`).
3. The bookmark icon's filled-state reflects whether the extension is in **any** collection (membership set non-empty).

The popover uses Reka UI's `Popover` (already a project dep through shadcn-vue). Localize labels via `useI18n()`.

### `SaveButton.vue` (existing — replace internals)

Becomes a thin wrapper that opens `SaveToCollectionPopover`. Keeps the same props/markup contract so callers in `ExtHero` don't change. The 3-second "Saved" transient is replaced by the more honest "is it in any collection" indicator.

### Profile section — `app/components/profile/SectionCollections.vue` (new) + `SectionRail.vue` (extend)

Add `"collections"` to the section enum in `SectionRail.vue`, `server/api/internal/profile/section.get.ts`, and `shared/types`. The new component lists user collections in cards: name (bilingual), item count, visibility chip (private/public), updated-at. Each card links to `/collections/<slug>`. Inline actions: rename, delete, toggle visibility, copy public link.

Rename/move existing `SectionSaved.vue`? Keep it for now under a separate `"saved"` tab — many users have built up muscle memory. Mark in a follow-up issue whether to fold it into `SectionCollections.vue` once the new feature lands.

### Pages

- `app/pages/collections/index.vue` (new) — Public browse. Fetches `/api/internal/collections/public`. Grid of `CollectionCard` (new shared component under `app/components/collection/`). Empty state when no public collections exist. Locale-aware via `useLocalePath`. Pagination via query string.
- `app/pages/collections/[slug].vue` (new) — Detail. SSR-fetches the collection. Renders header (name, description, owner, visibility chip), item grid using existing extension-card primitives (reuse from `SectionSaved.vue` or `app/components/extension/ExtensionCard.vue` if it exists), and an owner-only edit/delete/visibility-toggle bar. Toggling visibility makes a PATCH; toggling shows a "Copy link" affordance once public.

### Navigation

Add a "Collections" link to the TopBar (between Extensions and the user menu) pointing at `/collections`. Hidden when logged out? No — collections browse is public.

### i18n

Extend `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json` under existing namespaces:

- `profile.sections.collections` — sidebar label.
- `profile.emptyCollections.{title,body}` — empty state.
- `collections.browse.{title,subtitle,empty}` — `/collections` page copy.
- `collections.detail.{visibilityPrivate,visibilityPublic,owner,itemsHeading,empty,copyLink,linkCopied,confirmDelete}`
- `collections.actions.{create,rename,delete,makePublic,makePrivate,save,cancel}`
- `collections.form.{namePlaceholder,nameZhPlaceholder,descriptionPlaceholder,descriptionZhPlaceholder,visibilityHelp}`
- `common.saveToCollection`, `common.newCollection`, `common.added`, `common.removed`

Chinese translations included in the same commits.

## Files to create

```
shared/db/schema/collection.ts                            # extend
shared/validators/collection.ts                           # new
shared/types.ts                                           # extend (ProfileCollectionRow, CollectionVisibility)
server/utils/collections/shortcode.ts                     # new
server/utils/queries/collections.ts                       # new
server/utils/queries/profile.ts                           # extend (getCollectionsForUser)
server/api/internal/collections/index.get.ts              # new
server/api/internal/collections/index.post.ts             # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug].get.ts             # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug].patch.ts           # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug].delete.ts          # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug]/items.post.ts      # new
server/api/internal/collections/[slug]/items/[extensionId].delete.ts  # new
server/api/internal/collections/public.get.ts             # new
server/api/internal/collections/for-extension.get.ts      # new
server/api/internal/profile/section.get.ts                # extend (collections case)
app/pages/collections/index.vue                           # new
app/pages/collections/[slug].vue                          # new
app/components/extension/SaveToCollectionPopover.vue      # new
app/components/extension/SaveButton.vue                   # replace internals, keep props
app/components/collection/CollectionCard.vue              # new
app/components/collection/CollectionForm.vue              # new (used in create/rename modal)
app/components/collection/VisibilityToggle.vue            # new
app/components/profile/SectionCollections.vue             # new
app/components/profile/SectionRail.vue                    # extend
app/components/layout/TopBar.vue                          # extend (nav link)
i18n/locales/en.json                                      # extend
i18n/locales/zh.json                                      # extend
drizzle/<next>_collections_v2.sql                         # generated + backfill
```

## Reuse existing code

- `requireUser(event)` from `server/utils/auth.ts` for all internal routes.
- `useDb()` from `server/utils/db.ts` for the Drizzle handle.
- `recordInstall` orchestration in `shared/installs/record.ts` — extract its inline `getOrCreateSavedCollection` and reuse from the new collections queries. Keep `recordInstall` semantics unchanged.
- `getSavedForUser` in `server/utils/queries/profile.ts` — stays; the new `getCollectionsForUser` is additive.
- `app/components/profile/SectionSaved.vue` row layout — copy the card structure into `CollectionCard.vue` and `SectionCollections.vue`.
- Zod validators in `shared/validators/profile.ts` for shape, naming, and `_MAX` constant style.
- Reka UI `Popover` primitive — already wired through shadcn-vue (`app/components/ui/`) — use for the picker.

## Out of scope (deferred)

- No `/api/v1/collections/*` public registry endpoints. CLI cannot install-by-collection.
- No collection-level ratings, comments, or fork/clone.
- No org-scoped or department-scoped collections (only user-owned). Multi-tenancy hook exists on `collections.ownerUserId`; org ownership can be added later by introducing `ownerOrgId`.
- No featured/curated collections on the home page. The home featured hero stays as-is.
- No `unlisted` third visibility state — can be added later by extending the enum without breaking rows.

## Verification

End-to-end manual flow (run `bun run dev`, sign in as a test user):

1. **Schema** — `bun run db:generate` produces a clean migration; `bun run db:migrate` applies it; existing `saved` rows now have non-null `slug`, `visibility='private'`, item counts unchanged.
2. **Bookmark picker** — Open any extension detail page. Click bookmark → popover shows "Saved" pinned at top. Tick → row appears in Profile → Saved. Untick → row disappears.
3. **Create collection** — In the popover, "+ New collection" → name "Test Pack" → it gains a checkbox, ticking adds the current extension.
4. **Profile section** — Profile → Collections shows "Test Pack" (1 item) and "Saved" tagged as system.
5. **Detail page** — Click "Test Pack" card → lands on `/collections/<shortcode>` with the extension listed. Owner-only edit bar visible.
6. **Visibility toggle** — Toggle Test Pack to Public. URL stays the same. `publishedAt` populated. Open in a private window (signed out) → page loads, no edit bar, item visible.
7. **Browse** — Sign out, visit `/collections` → "Test Pack" appears in the public browse grid with owner name.
8. **Delete** — Back as owner, delete Test Pack → confirms, removes from profile + browse. Confirm `collection_items` cascade fired (no orphans).
9. **System collection guard** — Try `DELETE /api/internal/collections/<saved-slug>` directly via devtools → 422 with `system_collection_locked`.
10. **i18n** — Switch locale via TopBar. Every new string renders in zh; nothing falls back to a missing-key marker.

Automated coverage (added in the same PR series):

- `shared/validators/collection.test.ts` — Zod input cases (length bounds, visibility enum).
- `server/utils/queries/collections.test.ts` — integration test against the test DB: create + add + visibility toggle + browse + delete + system-collection guard.
- `shared/installs/record.test.ts` — keep green; confirms the extract-and-reuse of `getOrCreateSavedCollection` didn't regress install events.

Phase/commit slicing (per `docs/plan.md` §13):

1. `feat(p2-db): extend collections schema with slug + visibility + description` — schema + migration + backfill.
2. `feat(p9-collections): collections query layer + shortcode helper` — `server/utils/queries/collections.ts`, `shortcode.ts`, extract `getOrCreateSavedCollection`.
3. `feat(p9-collections): internal collections API routes` — all `server/api/internal/collections/*` routes + validators.
4. `feat(p9-collections): save-to-collection popover + SaveButton rewire` — UI for picker + popover.
5. `feat(p9-collections): /collections browse + detail pages` — public-facing pages + nav link.
6. `feat(p9-collections): profile collections section` — `SectionCollections` + section.get.ts wiring.
7. `feat(i18n): collections strings (en + zh)` — locale bundles.

Each step ends green on the validate pipeline before the next begins.

1	// Pure decision for the install flow's "which version to record" step.
2	// Same testability story as `shared/extensions/state.ts` — the orchestrator
3	// loads the candidate list and passes it in; this module just decides.
4	
5	export interface VersionCandidate {
6	  version: string
7	  publishedAt: Date | null
8	  createdAt: Date
9	}
10	
11	export type VersionPick =
12	  | { ok: true; version: string }
13	  | { ok: false; error: "no_published_version" }
14	
15	// `candidates` must already be sorted newest-first
16	// (`publishedAt DESC NULLS LAST, createdAt DESC` — matches what
17	// `versionsRepo.listLatestReadyForExtension` returns).
18	//
19	// - If the caller passed a specific version, that wins (passthrough).
20	// - Otherwise pick the first candidate; if there is none, return an error
21	//   so the orchestrator can map it to its domain exception.
22	export function pickInstallVersion(input: {
23	  requested: string | undefined
24	  candidates: VersionCandidate[]
25	}): VersionPick {
26	  const { requested, candidates } = input
27	
28	  if (requested) return { ok: true, version: requested }
29	
30	  const latest = candidates[0]
31	  if (!latest) return { ok: false, error: "no_published_version" }
32	
33	  return { ok: true, version: latest.version }
34	}
35

168	);
169	
170	export const files = pgTable("files", {
171	  id: text().primaryKey(),
172	  extensionVersionId: text().references(() => extensionVersions.id, {
173	    onDelete: "set null",
174	  }),
175	  r2Key: text().notNull(),
176	  size: bigint({ mode: "bigint" }).notNull(),
177	  checksumSha256: text().notNull(),
178	  mimeType: text(),
179	  scanStatus: fileScanStatusEnum().notNull().default("pending"),
180	  scanReport: jsonb(),
181	  createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
182	});
183	
184	export const tags = pgTable("tags", {
185	  id: text().primaryKey(), // tag key, e.g. "real-time"
186	  labelEn: text().notNull(),
187	  labelZh: text().notNull(),
188	});
189	
190	export const extensionTags = pgTable(
191	  "extension_tags",
192	  {
193	    extensionId: text()
194	      .notNull()
195	      .references(() => extensions.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
196	    tagId: text()
197	      .notNull()
198	      .references(() => tags.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
199	  },
200	  (t) => [
201	    primaryKey({ columns: [t.extensionId, t.tagId] }),
202	    index("idx_extension_tags_tag_ext").on(t.tagId, t.extensionId),
203	  ],
204	);
205

1	import * as collectionsRepo from "~~/server/repositories/collections"
2	import * as extensionsRepo from "~~/server/repositories/extensions"
3	import * as installsRepo from "~~/server/repositories/installs"
4	import * as versionsRepo from "~~/server/repositories/versions"
5	import { pickInstallVersion } from "~~/shared/installs/record"
6	
7	import { useDb } from "./db"
8	
9	// Orchestrator over the installs/collections/extensions/versions repos. The
10	// "which version to install" decision is still inline here; commit 8
11	// extracts it into the pure `shared/installs/record.ts` decision module.
12	
13	export type ExtensionRef = { id: string } | { slug: string }
14	export type InstallSource = "web" | "cli"
15	export type InstallErrorCode = "extension_not_found" | "no_published_version"
16	
17	export class InstallError extends Error {
18	  readonly code: InstallErrorCode
19	  constructor(code: InstallErrorCode) {
20	    super(code)
21	    this.code = code
22	    this.name = "InstallError"
23	  }
24	}
25	
26	export interface RecordInstallParams {
27	  userId: string
28	  extension: ExtensionRef
29	  source: InstallSource
30	  version?: string | undefined
31	}
32	
33	export interface InstallRecord {
34	  installId: string
35	  isFirstInstall: boolean
36	  version: string
37	}
38	
39	export async function recordInstall(
40	  params: RecordInstallParams,
41	): Promise<InstallRecord> {
42	  const { userId, extension, source } = params
43	  const db = useDb()
44	
45	  // Resolve the extension. `findBySlug` is `onlyPublished: true` by default,
46	  // which would hide drafts — the install flow is only allowed against
47	  // published extensions anyway, so the default is what we want.
48	  const ext =
49	    "id" in extension
50	      ? await extensionsRepo.findById(db, extension.id)
51	      : await extensionsRepo.findBySlug(db, extension.slug)
52	  if (!ext) throw new InstallError("extension_not_found")
53	  const extensionId = ext.id
54	
55	  // Resolve which version to install. The orchestrator only loads the
56	  // candidate list when it's actually needed (`requested` is undefined).
57	  const candidates = params.version
58	    ? []
59	    : await versionsRepo.listLatestReadyForExtension(db, extensionId)
60	  const pick = pickInstallVersion({ requested: params.version, candidates })
61	  if (!pick.ok) throw new InstallError(pick.error)
62	  const version = pick.version
63	
64	  const { id: installedColId } = await collectionsRepo.getOrCreateSystem(
65	    db,
66	    userId,
67	    "installed",
68	  )
69	  const installId = crypto.randomUUID()
70	  const resolvedVersion = version
71	
72	  const isFirstInstall = await db.transaction(async (tx) => {
73	    const prior = await installsRepo.findByUserAndExtension(
74	      tx,
75	      userId,
76	      extensionId,
77	    )
78	    const isFirst = prior === null
79	
80	    await installsRepo.insertInstall(tx, {
81	      id: installId,
82	      userId,
83	      extensionId,
84	      version: resolvedVersion,
85	      source,
86	    })
87	    await collectionsRepo.addItem(tx, installedColId, extensionId)
88	    await extensionsRepo.incrementDownloads(tx, extensionId)
89	
90	    return isFirst
91	  })
92	
93	  return { installId, isFirstInstall, version: resolvedVersion }
94	}
95

1	import { and, eq } from "drizzle-orm"
2	
3	import { collectionItems, collections } from "~~/shared/db/schema"
4	
5	import type { Transactable } from "./types"
6	
7	// `collections` + `collection_items` table accessor. The install
8	// orchestrator uses these to make sure every install also drops a row into
9	// the user's "Installed" system collection.
10	
11	export type SystemKind = "installed" | "saved"
12	
13	const SYSTEM_KIND_LABEL: Record<SystemKind, string> = {
14	  installed: "Installed",
15	  saved: "Saved",
16	}
17	
18	// Look up (or create) the per-user system collection of the given kind.
19	// Idempotent: safe to call concurrently — the worst case is two rows of
20	// the same kind for the same user, which the install flow then both
21	// `addItem`s into without breakage. If that race ever shows up in prod,
22	// add a partial unique index on (owner_user_id, system_kind).
23	export async function getOrCreateSystem(
24	  db: Transactable,
25	  userId: string,
26	  kind: SystemKind,
27	): Promise<{ id: string }> {
28	  const existing = await db
29	    .select({ id: collections.id })
30	    .from(collections)
31	    .where(
32	      and(
33	        eq(collections.ownerUserId, userId),
34	        eq(collections.systemKind, kind),
35	      ),
36	    )
37	    .limit(1)
38	  if (existing[0]) return existing[0]
39	
40	  const id = crypto.randomUUID()
41	  await db.insert(collections).values({
42	    id,
43	    ownerUserId: userId,
44	    name: SYSTEM_KIND_LABEL[kind],
45	    systemKind: kind,
46	  })
47	  return { id }
48	}
49	
50	export async function addItem(
51	  db: Transactable,
52	  collectionId: string,
53	  extensionId: string,
54	): Promise<void> {
55	  await db
56	    .insert(collectionItems)
57	    .values({ collectionId, extensionId })
58	    .onConflictDoNothing()
59	}
60
