1	import type { ExtractTablesWithRelations } from "drizzle-orm"
2	import type { PgDatabase, PgQueryResultHKT } from "drizzle-orm/pg-core"
3	
4	import type * as schema from "~~/shared/db/schema"
5	
6	type Schema = typeof schema
7	
8	// `Transactable` covers any concrete Drizzle Postgres database — both the
9	// production `postgres-js` driver and the PGlite driver used in integration
10	// tests — *and* an open transaction handle (`PgTransaction` extends
11	// `PgDatabase`). Repository functions accept this and never branch on the
12	// driver, so an orchestrator can pass either `useDb()` or a `tx` callback
13	// argument and the same repo code works for both.
14	export type Transactable = PgDatabase<
15	  PgQueryResultHKT,
16	  Schema,
17	  ExtractTablesWithRelations<Schema>
18	>
19

1	import { and, count, desc, eq, ne, sql } from "drizzle-orm"
2	
3	import { extensions, extensionTags } from "~~/shared/db/schema"
4	import {
5	  buildExtensionOrder,
6	  buildExtensionWhere,
7	} from "~~/shared/search/query"
8	import {
9	  PAGE_SIZE,
10	  pageOffset,
11	  type Filters,
12	} from "~~/shared/validators/filters"
13	
14	import type { Transactable } from "./types"
15	
16	// `extensions` table accessor. Every function takes a `Transactable` as the
17	// first arg so it composes with `db.transaction(tx => ...)` and with the
18	// PGlite-backed integration test DB.
19	
20	// Listing select — used by browse list, home featured, and admin list. Keeps
21	// tag aggregation inlined as a Postgres `array_agg`, matching the previous
22	// `server/utils/queries/extensions.ts` shape so consumer code is a drop-in
23	// rename.
24	const listSelect = {
25	  id: extensions.id,
26	  slug: extensions.slug,
27	  category: extensions.category,
28	  badge: extensions.badge,
29	  scope: extensions.scope,
30	  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
31	  subCat: extensions.subCat,
32	  l2: extensions.l2,
33	  deptId: extensions.deptId,
34	  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
35	  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
36	  name: extensions.name,
37	  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
38	  tagline: extensions.tagline,
39	  taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
40	  description: extensions.description,
41	  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
42	  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
43	  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
44	  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
45	}
46	
47	const detailSelect = {
48	  id: extensions.id,
49	  slug: extensions.slug,
50	  category: extensions.category,
51	  badge: extensions.badge,
52	  scope: extensions.scope,
53	  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
54	  subCat: extensions.subCat,
55	  l2: extensions.l2,
56	  deptId: extensions.deptId,
57	  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
58	  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
59	  name: extensions.name,
60	  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
61	  tagline: extensions.tagline,
62	  taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
63	  description: extensions.description,
64	  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
65	  readmeMd: extensions.readmeMd,
66	  homepageUrl: extensions.homepageUrl,
67	  repoUrl: extensions.repoUrl,
68	  licenseSpdx: extensions.licenseSpdx,
69	  compatibilityJson: extensions.compatibilityJson,
70	  permissions: extensions.permissions,
71	  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
72	  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
73	  ratingsCount: extensions.ratingsCount,
74	  publishedAt: extensions.publishedAt,
75	  publisherUserId: extensions.publisherUserId,
76	  ownerOrgId: extensions.ownerOrgId,
77	  visibility: extensions.visibility,
78	  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
79	}
80	
81	const relatedSelect = {
82	  id: extensions.id,
83	  slug: extensions.slug,
84	  name: extensions.name,
85	  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
86	  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
87	  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
88	  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
89	  badge: extensions.badge,
90	  deptId: extensions.deptId,
91	  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
92	}
93	
94	export async function findManyForList(
95	  db: Transactable,
96	  filters: Filters,
97	  userDeptId?: string,
98	) {
99	  const where = buildExtensionWhere(db, filters, userDeptId)
100	  const order = buildExtensionOrder(filters.sort)
101	
102	  return db
103	    .select(listSelect)
104	    .from(extensions)
105	    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
106	    .where(where)
107	    .groupBy(extensions.id)
108	    .orderBy(...order)
109	    .limit(PAGE_SIZE)
110	    .offset(pageOffset(filters.page))
111	}
112	
113	export async function countFiltered(
114	  db: Transactable,
115	  filters: Filters,
116	  userDeptId?: string,
117	): Promise<number> {
118	  const where = buildExtensionWhere(db, filters, userDeptId)
119	  const [row] = await db
120	    .select({ count: count() })
121	    .from(extensions)
122	    .where(where)
123	  return Number(row?.count ?? 0)
124	}
125	
126	export async function findFeatured(db: Transactable) {
127	  // Prefer the hand-curated row. Fall back to top-downloaded so the home
128	  // hero is never empty.
129	  //
130	  // Both queries cap to one row; without a stable tiebreaker the choice
131	  // would flap on ties (two curated rows published at the same instant,
132	  // or two extensions with identical download counts). Sort by id last so
133	  // the home hero is deterministic across requests.
134	  const [curated] = await db
135	    .select(listSelect)
136	    .from(extensions)
137	    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
138	    .where(
139	      and(
140	        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
141	        eq(extensions.featured, true),
142	      ),
143	    )
144	    .groupBy(extensions.id)
145	    .orderBy(desc(extensions.publishedAt), desc(extensions.id))
146	    .limit(1)
147	  if (curated) return curated
148	
149	  const [fallback] = await db
150	    .select(listSelect)
151	    .from(extensions)
152	    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
153	    .where(eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
154	    .groupBy(extensions.id)
155	    .orderBy(
156	      desc(extensions.downloadsCount),
157	      desc(extensions.publishedAt),
158	      desc(extensions.id),
159	    )
160	    .limit(1)
161	  return fallback ?? null
162	}
163	
164	export async function findRelated(
165	  db: Transactable,
166	  extensionId: string,
167	  category: "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins",
168	) {
169	  return db
170	    .select(relatedSelect)
171	    .from(extensions)
172	    .where(
173	      and(
174	        eq(extensions.category, category),
175	        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
176	        ne(extensions.id, extensionId),
177	      ),
178	    )
179	    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
180	    .limit(4)
181	}
182	
183	export async function findBySlug(
184	  db: Transactable,
185	  slug: string,
186	  opts: { onlyPublished?: boolean } = {},
187	) {
188	  const onlyPublished = opts.onlyPublished ?? true
189	  const where = onlyPublished
190	    ? and(eq(extensions.slug, slug), eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
191	    : eq(extensions.slug, slug)
192	
193	  const [row] = await db
194	    .select(detailSelect)
195	    .from(extensions)
196	    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
197	    .where(where)
198	    .groupBy(extensions.id)
199	  return row ?? null
200	}
201	
202	export async function findById(db: Transactable, id: string) {
203	  const [row] = await db
204	    .select(detailSelect)
205	    .from(extensions)
206	    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
207	    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
208	    .groupBy(extensions.id)
209	  return row ?? null
210	}
211	
212	export interface VisibilityUpdate {
213	  visibility: "draft" | "published" | "archived"
214	  publishedAt: Date | null
215	}
216	
217	export async function updateVisibility(
218	  db: Transactable,
219	  id: string,
220	  payload: VisibilityUpdate,
221	): Promise<void> {
222	  await db.update(extensions).set(payload).where(eq(extensions.id, id))
223	}
224	
225	export async function incrementDownloads(
226	  db: Transactable,
227	  id: string,
228	): Promise<void> {
229	  await db
230	    .update(extensions)
231	    .set({ downloadsCount: sql`${extensions.downloadsCount} + 1` })
232	    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
233	}
234

import { and, count, desc, eq, ne, sql } from "drizzle-orm"

import { extensions, extensionTags } from "~~/shared/db/schema"
import {
  buildExtensionOrder,
  buildExtensionWhere,
} from "~~/shared/search/query"
import {
  PAGE_SIZE,
  pageOffset,
  type Filters,
} from "~~/shared/validators/filters"

import type { Transactable } from "./types"

// `extensions` table accessor. Every function takes a `Transactable` as the
// first arg so it composes with `db.transaction(tx => ...)` and with the
// PGlite-backed integration test DB.

// Listing select — used by browse list, home featured, and admin list. Keeps
// tag aggregation inlined as a Postgres `array_agg`, matching the previous
// `server/utils/queries/extensions.ts` shape so consumer code is a drop-in
// rename.
const listSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  category: extensions.category,
  badge: extensions.badge,
  scope: extensions.scope,
  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
  subCat: extensions.subCat,
  l2: extensions.l2,
  deptId: extensions.deptId,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  tagline: extensions.tagline,
  taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
  description: extensions.description,
  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
}

const detailSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  category: extensions.category,
  badge: extensions.badge,
  scope: extensions.scope,
  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
  subCat: extensions.subCat,
  l2: extensions.l2,
  deptId: extensions.deptId,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  tagline: extensions.tagline,
  taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
  description: extensions.description,
  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
  readmeMd: extensions.readmeMd,
  homepageUrl: extensions.homepageUrl,
  repoUrl: extensions.repoUrl,
  licenseSpdx: extensions.licenseSpdx,
  compatibilityJson: extensions.compatibilityJson,
  permissions: extensions.permissions,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
  ratingsCount: extensions.ratingsCount,
  publishedAt: extensions.publishedAt,
  publisherUserId: extensions.publisherUserId,
  ownerOrgId: extensions.ownerOrgId,
  visibility: extensions.visibility,
  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
}

const relatedSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
  badge: extensions.badge,
  deptId: extensions.deptId,
  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
}

export async function findManyForList(
  db: Transactable,
  filters: Filters,
  userDeptId?: string,
) {
  const where = buildExtensionWhere(db, filters, userDeptId)
  const order = buildExtensionOrder(filters.sort)

  return db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(where)
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(...order)
    .limit(PAGE_SIZE)
    .offset(pageOffset(filters.page))
}

export async function countFiltered(
  db: Transactable,
  filters: Filters,
  userDeptId?: string,
): Promise<number> {
  const where = buildExtensionWhere(db, filters, userDeptId)
  const [row] = await db
    .select({ count: count() })
    .from(extensions)
    .where(where)
  return Number(row?.count ?? 0)
}

export async function findFeatured(db: Transactable) {
  // Prefer the hand-curated row. Fall back to top-downloaded so the home
  // hero is never empty.
  //
  // Both queries cap to one row; without a stable tiebreaker the choice
  // would flap on ties (two curated rows published at the same instant,
  // or two extensions with identical download counts). Sort by id last so
  // the home hero is deterministic across requests.
  const [curated] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(
      and(
        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
        eq(extensions.featured, true),
      ),
    )
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.publishedAt), desc(extensions.id))
    .limit(1)
  if (curated) return curated

  const [fallback] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(
      desc(extensions.downloadsCount),
      desc(extensions.publishedAt),
      desc(extensions.id),
    )
    .limit(1)
  return fallback ?? null
}

export async function findRelated(
  db: Transactable,
  extensionId: string,
  category: "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins",
) {
  return db
    .select(relatedSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .where(
      and(
        eq(extensions.category, category),
        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
        ne(extensions.id, extensionId),
      ),
    )
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
    .limit(4)
}

export async function findBySlug(
  db: Transactable,
  slug: string,
  opts: { onlyPublished?: boolean } = {},
) {
  const onlyPublished = opts.onlyPublished ?? true
  const where = onlyPublished
    ? and(eq(extensions.slug, slug), eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    : eq(extensions.slug, slug)

  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(where)
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
  return row ?? null
}

export async function findById(db: Transactable, id: string) {
  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
  return row ?? null
}

export interface VisibilityUpdate {
  visibility: "draft" | "published" | "archived"
  publishedAt: Date | null
}

export async function updateVisibility(
  db: Transactable,
  id: string,
  payload: VisibilityUpdate,
): Promise<void> {
  await db.update(extensions).set(payload).where(eq(extensions.id, id))
}

export async function incrementDownloads(
  db: Transactable,
  id: string,
): Promise<void> {
  await db
    .update(extensions)
    .set({ downloadsCount: sql`${extensions.downloadsCount} + 1` })
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
}

import { z } from "zod"

// Input schemas for the collections feature. Mirrors the lightweight,
// constant-led style used in `profile.ts` so the API handlers can call
// `.parse(raw)` directly and the UI can import the same `_MAX` constants for
// inline character counters.
export const COLLECTION_NAME_MIN = 1
export const COLLECTION_NAME_MAX = 100
export const COLLECTION_DESC_MAX = 500

export const CollectionVisibility = z.enum(["private", "public"])
export type CollectionVisibility = z.infer<typeof CollectionVisibility>

export const CreateCollectionInput = z.object({
  name: z.string().trim().min(COLLECTION_NAME_MIN).max(COLLECTION_NAME_MAX),
  nameZh: z
    .string()
    .trim()
    .max(COLLECTION_NAME_MAX)
    .optional()
    .transform((v) => (v && v.length > 0 ? v : undefined)),
  description: z
    .string()
    .trim()
    .max(COLLECTION_DESC_MAX)
    .optional()
    .transform((v) => (v && v.length > 0 ? v : undefined)),
  descriptionZh: z
    .string()
    .trim()
    .max(COLLECTION_DESC_MAX)
    .optional()
    .transform((v) => (v && v.length > 0 ? v : undefined)),
  visibility: CollectionVisibility.default("private"),
})

export type CreateCollectionInput = z.infer<typeof CreateCollectionInput>

export const UpdateCollectionInput = CreateCollectionInput.partial()
export type UpdateCollectionInput = z.infer<typeof UpdateCollectionInput>

export const AddItemInput = z.object({
  extensionId: z.string().min(1),
})
export type AddItemInput = z.infer<typeof AddItemInput>

1	import * as extensionsRepo from "~~/server/repositories/extensions"
2	import * as versionsRepo from "~~/server/repositories/versions"
3	
4	export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
5	  const query = getQuery(event)
6	  const slug = typeof query.slug === "string" ? query.slug : null
7	  if (!slug) throw createError({ statusCode: 400, statusMessage: "slug required" })
8	
9	  const db = useDb()
10	  const ext = await extensionsRepo.findBySlug(db, slug)
11	  if (!ext) throw createError({ statusCode: 404, statusMessage: "not_found" })
12	
13	  const [versions, related] = await Promise.all([
14	    versionsRepo.listForExtension(db, ext.id),
15	    extensionsRepo.findRelated(db, ext.id, ext.category),
16	  ])
17	
18	  return { ext, versions, related }
19	})

export type Locale = "en" | "zh"

  key: string
  l2: string[]
}

  key: FuncCatKey
  l1: FuncTaxonomyL1[]
}

export interface TagLabel {
  en: string
  zh: string
}

export interface Department {
  id: string
  name: string
  nameZh: string
  children?: Department[]
}

  id: number
  dept: string
  tags: string[]
  name: string
  nameZh: string

  badge: ExtensionBadge | null
  downloads: number
  stars: number
  color: string
  icon: string
  funcCat: FuncCatKey
  subCat: string
  scope: ExtensionScope
  desc: string
  descZh: string
}

export interface Collection {
  id: string
  name: string
  nameZh: string
  count: number
}

export interface CreatorFacet {
  id: string

  email: string
  count: number
}

export interface PublisherFacet {
  id: string
  name: string

  slug: string
  count: number
}

export interface TagFacet {
  id: string
  labelEn: string

  count: number
}

export interface ProfileStats {

  publishedCount: number

}

  category: string

<script setup lang="ts">
import type { HTMLAttributes } from "vue"
import {
  PopoverContent,
  type PopoverContentEmits,
  type PopoverContentProps,
  PopoverPortal,
  useForwardPropsEmits,
} from "reka-ui"
import { cn } from "~/lib/utils"

const props = withDefaults(
  defineProps<PopoverContentProps & { class?: HTMLAttributes["class"] }>(),
  { align: "center", sideOffset: 4 },
)
const emits = defineEmits<PopoverContentEmits>()

const forwarded = useForwardPropsEmits(
  computed(() => {
    const { class: _, ...rest } = props
    return rest
  }),
  emits,
)
</script>

<template>
  <PopoverPortal>
    <PopoverContent
      v-bind="forwarded"
      :class="cn(
        'z-50 w-72 rounded-md border border-(--color-border) bg-(--color-card) p-4 text-(--color-ink) shadow-md outline-none',
        'data-[state=open]:animate-in data-[state=closed]:animate-out data-[state=closed]:fade-out-0 data-[state=open]:fade-in-0 data-[state=closed]:zoom-out-95 data-[state=open]:zoom-in-95',
        props.class,
      )"
    >
      <slot />
    </PopoverContent>
  </PopoverPortal>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import type { HTMLAttributes } from "vue"
import { Check } from "lucide-vue-next"
import {
  CheckboxIndicator,
  CheckboxRoot,
  type CheckboxRootEmits,
  type CheckboxRootProps,
  useForwardPropsEmits,
} from "reka-ui"
import { cn } from "~/lib/utils"

const props = defineProps<CheckboxRootProps & { class?: HTMLAttributes["class"] }>()
const emits = defineEmits<CheckboxRootEmits>()

const forwarded = useForwardPropsEmits(
  computed(() => {
    const { class: _, ...rest } = props
    return rest
  }),
  emits,
)
</script>

<template>
  <CheckboxRoot
    v-bind="forwarded"
    :class="cn(
      'peer size-4 shrink-0 rounded-sm border border-(--color-border) shadow focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-(--color-accent)/40 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50',

      props.class,
    )"
  >
    <CheckboxIndicator class="flex items-center justify-center text-current">
      <Check class="size-3" />
    </CheckboxIndicator>
  </CheckboxRoot>
</template>

file.content:
<script setup lang="ts">
import type { HTMLAttributes, InputHTMLAttributes } from "vue"
import { cn } from "~/lib/utils"

const props = defineProps<{
  modelValue?: string | number | null
  type?: InputHTMLAttributes["type"]
  class?: HTMLAttributes["class"]
}>()

const emit = defineEmits<{
  (e: "update:modelValue", value: string | number | null): void
}>()

function onInput(event: Event) {
  const el = event.target as HTMLInputElement
  if (props.type === "number") {
    emit("update:modelValue", el.value === "" ? null : Number(el.value))
    return
  }
  emit("update:modelValue", el.value)
}
</script>

<template>
  <input
    :type="props.type ?? 'text'"
    :value="modelValue ?? ''"
    :class="cn(
      'flex h-9 w-full rounded-md border border-(--color-border) bg-(--color-card) px-3 py-1 text-sm text-(--color-ink) placeholder:text-(--color-ink-muted) shadow-sm transition-colors',
      'focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-(--color-accent)/40 focus-visible:border-(--color-accent)/60',
      'disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50',
      props.class,
    )"
    @input="onInput"
  >
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Activity, Bookmark, Download, FileText, Settings, Upload } from "lucide-vue-next"

export type ProfileSectionKey =
  | "installed"
  | "published"
  | "drafts"
  | "saved"
  | "activity"
  | "settings"

defineProps<{
  active: ProfileSectionKey
}>()

const { t } = useI18n()
const localePath = useLocalePath()

const items: Array<{ key: ProfileSectionKey; icon: typeof Download }> = [
  { key: "installed", icon: Download },
  { key: "published", icon: Upload },
  { key: "drafts", icon: FileText },
  { key: "saved", icon: Bookmark },
  { key: "activity", icon: Activity },
  { key: "settings", icon: Settings },
]

function href(key: ProfileSectionKey): string {
  return `${localePath("/profile")}?section=${key}`
}
</script>

<template>
  <nav class="flex flex-col gap-1">
    <NuxtLink
      v-for="item in items"
      :key="item.key"
      :to="href(item.key)"
      class="flex items-center gap-2.5 rounded-md px-3 py-2 text-sm transition-colors"
      :class="active === item.key
        ? 'bg-(--color-sidebar) text-(--color-ink) font-medium'
        : 'text-(--color-ink-muted) hover:bg-(--color-sidebar)/60 hover:text-(--color-ink)'"
    >
      <component :is="item.icon" :size="16" aria-hidden="true" />
      {{ t(`profile.sections.${item.key}`) }}
    </NuxtLink>
  </nav>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Activity, Bookmark, Download, FileText, Settings, Upload } from "lucide-vue-next"

export type ProfileSectionKey =
  | "installed"
  | "published"
  | "drafts"
  | "saved"
  | "activity"
  | "settings"

defineProps<{
  active: ProfileSectionKey
}>()

const { t } = useI18n()
const localePath = useLocalePath()

const items: Array<{ key: ProfileSectionKey; icon: typeof Download }> = [
  { key: "installed", icon: Download },
  { key: "published", icon: Upload },
  { key: "drafts", icon: FileText },
  { key: "saved", icon: Bookmark },
  { key: "activity", icon: Activity },
  { key: "settings", icon: Settings },
]

function href(key: ProfileSectionKey): string {
  return `${localePath("/profile")}?section=${key}`
}
</script>

<template>
  <nav class="flex flex-col gap-1">
    <NuxtLink
      v-for="item in items"
      :key="item.key"
      :to="href(item.key)"
      class="flex items-center gap-2.5 rounded-md px-3 py-2 text-sm transition-colors"
      :class="active === item.key
        ? 'bg-(--color-sidebar) text-(--color-ink) font-medium'
        : 'text-(--color-ink-muted) hover:bg-(--color-sidebar)/60 hover:text-(--color-ink)'"
    >
      <component :is="item.icon" :size="16" aria-hidden="true" />
      {{ t(`profile.sections.${item.key}`) }}
    </NuxtLink>
  </nav>
</template>

input.description: Locate key insertion points in zh.json

content.subject: Extend collections schema (slug, visibility, description, publishedAt, updatedAt)
content.description: Edit shared/db/schema/collection.ts to add the new columns + enum + indexes per plan.

content.subject: Generate + adjust drizzle migration with slug backfill
content.description: Run bun run db:generate, inspect output, add the slug backfill block before NOT NULL/UNIQUE constraints.

content.subject: Add collection validators (shared/validators/collection.ts)
content.description: Create + Update + AddItem zod schemas mirroring profile.ts style.

content.subject: Shortcode helper + collections query layer
content.description: Add server/utils/collections/shortcode.ts and server/utils/queries/collections.ts with listForOwner/getBySlug/listPublicPaged/getMembershipForExtension/create/update/remove/addItem/removeItem and CollectionError class. Extract getOrCreateSavedCollection.

content.subject: Wire shared/installs/record.ts to use extracted helper
content.description: Keep recordInstall semantics; have it import the new shared helper rather than inline-creating the saved collection.

content.subject: Internal collections API routes
content.description: All 9 routes under server/api/internal/collections/: index.get/post, [slug].get/patch/delete, [slug]/items.post, [slug]/items/[extensionId].delete, public.get, for-extension.get.

content.subject: Extend profile section endpoint + add getCollectionsForUser
content.description: Add collections case to server/api/internal/profile/section.get.ts, add getCollectionsForUser query, update SectionKey/SectionResponse and shared/types.

content.subject: SaveToCollectionPopover + SaveButton rewire
content.description: New popover component using Reka UI Popover; SaveButton becomes a thin trigger keeping same prop contract.

content.subject: Collection pages (browse + detail) + nav link
content.description: app/pages/collections/index.vue (browse), app/pages/collections/[slug].vue (detail), CollectionCard + CollectionForm + VisibilityToggle components, TopBar nav link.

content.subject: Profile SectionCollections + SectionRail update
content.description: New SectionCollections component + add 'collections' to SectionRail and profile.vue wiring.

content.subject: i18n strings (en + zh)
content.description: Add all collections.*, profile.sections.collections, profile.emptyCollections.*, common.* keys to both locales.

content.subject: Run validate pipeline (lint + typecheck + tests)
content.description: bun run lint, bun run typecheck, bun test — green before declaring done.

content.subject: Extend profile section endpoint + add getCollectionsForUser
content.description: Add collections case to server/api/internal/profile/section.get.ts, add getCollectionsForUser query, update SectionKey/SectionResponse and shared/types.

content.subject: SaveToCollectionPopover + SaveButton rewire
content.description: New popover component using Reka UI Popover; SaveButton becomes a thin trigger keeping same prop contract.

content.subject: Collection pages (browse + detail) + nav link
content.description: app/pages/collections/index.vue (browse), app/pages/collections/[slug].vue (detail), CollectionCard + CollectionForm + VisibilityToggle components, TopBar nav link.

content.subject: Profile SectionCollections + SectionRail update
content.description: New SectionCollections component + add 'collections' to SectionRail and profile.vue wiring.

content.subject: i18n strings (en + zh)
content.description: Add all collections.*, profile.sections.collections, profile.emptyCollections.*, common.* keys to both locales.

content.subject: Run validate pipeline (lint + typecheck + tests)
content.description: bun run lint, bun run typecheck, bun test — green before declaring done.

content.content: Updated task #12 status

content.text:
Lint, typecheck, and 242 tests all green. Summary of what landed:

**Schema (`shared/db/schema/collection.ts` + `drizzle/0007_wakeful_cloak.sql`)**
- New columns on `collections`: `slug` (10-char base36 shortcode, unique), `description`/`descriptionZh`, `visibility` (`private`/`public` enum), `publishedAt`, `updatedAt`.
- Indexes on `ownerUserId` and `(visibility, publishedAt DESC)`.
- Hand-edited migration adds `slug` nullable → backfills via `md5(id||rand())` → flips to NOT NULL + UNIQUE so the upgrade is safe on existing rows.

**Server**
- `server/utils/collections/shortcode.ts` — uniform base36 generator + DB-checked unique wrapper.
- `server/utils/collections/errors.ts` — `mapCollectionError` HTTP mapping.
- `server/repositories/collections.ts` — extended with `findBySlug`, `create`, `update` (handles first private→public publishedAt stamp), `remove`, `addItemBySlug`, `removeItemBySlug`, `membershipForExtension`, `CollectionError`. System collections rejected from edit/delete with `system_collection_locked`.
- `server/utils/queries/collections.ts` — `listForOwner` (pins `saved` at top, hides `installed`), `listPublicPaged`, `listItems`, `getOwnerName`.
- `server/utils/installs.ts` test stub updated to match new `{id, slug}` return.

**API routes (POST-based mutations to match codebase convention)**
- `GET/POST /api/internal/collections`
- `GET /api/internal/collections/[slug]` (ACL: public visible to anyone, private to owner only)
- `POST /api/internal/collections/[slug]/update`
- `POST /api/internal/collections/[slug]/delete`
- `POST /api/internal/collections/[slug]/items`
- `POST /api/internal/collections/[slug]/items/[extensionId]/remove`
- `GET /api/internal/collections/public` (paginated browse)
- `GET /api/internal/collection-memberships?extensionId=…` (named outside `[slug]` to dodge InternalApi type collisions)

**Validators** — `shared/validators/collection.ts` (Create / Update / AddItem zod schemas with trimming + empty-string→undefined coercion).

**UI**
- `SaveToCollectionPopover.vue` — checkbox list + inline "New collection" creator; "Saved" pinned at top; optimistic toggles with rollback. `SaveButton.vue` becomes a thin wrapper.
- `app/pages/collections/index.vue` — public browse grid with pagination.
- `app/pages/collections/[slug].vue` — detail page with owner-only edit/visibility/delete bar, copy-link affordance once public, item remove.
- `app/components/collection/{CollectionCard,CollectionForm}.vue` — shared card + form.
- `app/components/profile/SectionCollections.vue` + `SectionRail.vue` extended; "Collections" section added to profile.
- TopBar gets a `Collections` nav link between Explore and Publish.

**i18n** — `nav.collections`, `profile.sections.collections`, `profile.emptyCollections.*`, full `collections.*` namespace (browse, popover, form, actions, detail) in both `en.json` and `zh.json`.

The system `Saved` collection is preserved unchanged for `recordInstall`; new lazy-init goes through the same repository helper. Next step would be a smoke pass with `bun run dev` — and per the plan, the `/api/v1` public registry contract is untouched.

content.text: Signed in. Now exercising the complete flow: list collections (lazy-seeds Saved), pick an extension, create a public collection, add the item, verify visibility, and confirm browse pickup.

input.command:
echo "--- list collections (should lazy-seed Saved) ---"
curl -s -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections | head -c 400; echo
echo
echo "--- pick a published extension ---"
EXT=$(psql -t "postgresql://hutusi@localhost:5432/agentcenter" -c "select id from extensions where visibility='published' limit 1;" | tr -d ' ')
echo "EXT=$EXT"
echo
echo "--- create a public collection ---"
CREATED=$(curl -s -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"My SRE picks","nameZh":"SRE 精选","description":"Curated for on-call","visibility":"public"}')
echo "$CREATED"
SLUG=$(echo "$CREATED" | sed -n 's/.*"slug":"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/p')
echo "SLUG=$SLUG"
echo
echo "--- add the extension to it ---"
curl -s -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt -X POST "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections/$SLUG/items" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"extensionId\":\"$EXT\"}"; echo
echo
echo "--- fetch the detail page (owner view) ---"
curl -s -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections/$SLUG" | head -c 500; echo
echo
echo "--- membership lookup ---"
curl -s -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collection-memberships?extensionId=$EXT"; echo
echo
echo "--- public browse picks it up ---"
curl -s "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections/public" | head -c 500; echo
echo
echo "--- visitor (no cookie) can read the public collection ---"
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections/$SLUG"

content.text: The shell sed broke pretty-print parsing — slug came out empty so the add call hit `/collections//items`. Re-running with jq parsing.

input.command:
SLUG="olqomzsyw3"   # known from the prior create
EXT=$(psql -t "postgresql://hutusi@localhost:5432/agentcenter" -c "select id from extensions where visibility='published' limit 1;" | tr -d ' ')
echo "Re-using SLUG=$SLUG, EXT=$EXT"
echo
echo "--- add the extension to it ---"
curl -s -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt -X POST "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections/$SLUG/items" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"extensionId\":\"$EXT\"}"; echo
echo
echo "--- detail page as owner, item should now appear ---"
curl -s -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections/$SLUG" | jq '{collection: {slug, name, visibility, isOwner, itemCount}, itemCount: (.items|length), firstItem: .items[0]}'
echo
echo "--- detail as anonymous visitor (should succeed, public collection) ---"
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "anon GET: %{http_code}\n" "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections/$SLUG"
echo
echo "--- membership lookup now ---"
curl -s -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collection-memberships?extensionId=$EXT"; echo
echo
echo "--- flip to private + try anon again ---"
curl -s -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt -X POST "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections/$SLUG/update" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"visibility":"private"}'; echo
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "anon GET after private: %{http_code}\n" "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections/$SLUG"
echo
echo "--- try to delete the Saved system collection (should 422) ---"
SAVED_SLUG=$(curl -s -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections | jq -r '.rows[] | select(.systemKind=="saved") | .slug')
echo "SAVED_SLUG=$SAVED_SLUG"
curl -s -o /tmp/resp.json -w "delete-saved: %{http_code}\n" -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt -X POST "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections/$SAVED_SLUG/delete"
cat /tmp/resp.json | jq '.statusMessage // .message // .'

content.text:
Migration applied cleanly and the feature works end-to-end:

| Check | Result |
| --- | --- |
| `db:migrate` against existing DB | applied cleanly (0 system rows so no backfill needed) |
| Schema shape (slug NOT NULL UNIQUE, visibility enum, indexes) | matches the plan exactly |
| `GET /api/internal/collections` lazy-seeds Saved | ✓ |
| Create public collection (bilingual name + description) | returns slug `olqomzsyw3`, visibility `public`, publishedAt stamped |
| Add extension `ext-1` to the collection | ✓, idempotent |
| Owner GET sees `itemCount: 1`, the item, `isOwner: true` | ✓ |
| Anonymous GET on public collection | 200 |
| Flip to private → anonymous GET | 404 (no info leak) |
| publishedAt stays populated through visibility toggle | ✓ (stable browse sort) |
| Membership lookup `?extensionId=ext-1` | returns the collection id |
| `DELETE` the Saved system collection | 422 `system_collection_locked` |
| `/en/collections` SSR renders the browse card | "My SRE picks · Curated for on-call · Coll Tester · olqomzsyw3" |
| `/en/collections/olqomzsyw3` SSR renders detail | name + description + owner + item "Web Search Pro" |
| `/zh/collections/olqomzsyw3` shows the Chinese fields | "SRE 精选 · 网页搜索专业版 · 公开" |

Test user created during the run: `collections-test@example.com` / `testpass123` — owner of one public collection `olqomzsyw3`. Leaving it in the DB so you can sign in and poke the UI directly; remove with `delete from users where email = 'collections-test@example.com'` when done.

content.text:
A few natural next steps, in rough order of importance:

1. **You're on the wrong branch.** This work landed on `feat/home-no-sidebar-centered-layout` (the home redesign branch). Recommend cutting a fresh `feat/collections` branch off `main`, cherry-picking the changes there, and opening a PR from that — otherwise this gets entangled with the home work.
2. **Commit the slices.** Per `docs/plan.md` §13 + your CLAUDE.md, this is 6–7 coherent commits (schema+migration, query layer, API routes, popover+SaveButton, browse+detail pages, profile section, i18n). I haven't committed anything yet.
3. **Browser smoke test.** I verified the API + SSR markup but never actually clicked through the popover, "+ New collection", or the visibility toggle in a browser.
4. **Integration tests.** The plan mentioned `server/utils/queries/collections.test.ts` and `shared/validators/collection.test.ts` — neither exists yet.

Want me to start with #1 (rebranch and commit) — that's the only one that's risky to defer?

content.content:
## 13. Commit & PR rules

The original Next.js project locked "Conventional Commits, one coherent unit per commit, phase scope." That carries over verbatim. This section spells out the parts that were left informal — granularity, breaking-change handling for `/api/v1`, merge strategy, the per-commit vs. per-PR validate split, and the hook tooling.

### Commit message format

```
<type>(<scope>): <one-line summary, lowercase, no trailing period>

<body — optional. Why, not what. Wraps at 72 chars.
Reference incidents, surprising decisions, or invariants the diff doesn't show.>

<footer — optional. BREAKING CHANGE, Closes #N, …>
```

### Types

`feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `test`, `docs`, `chore`, `perf`, `revert`, `style`. Use the type that describes the dominant change in the commit. A commit that's "feat plus its tests" is `feat`, not split.

### Scopes

Two scope shapes used here:

- **Phase scope** during the v1 build: `p<N>-<short>` matching §10. Example: `feat(p3-browse): wire filter chips to query string`. Use this for any commit that lands a phase deliverable.
- **Feature scope** for hotfixes during v1 and all post-v1 work: a short noun for the area touched. Examples: `fix(filter-bar): preserve dept on locale switch`, `refactor(install-button): extract install state machine`, `chore(deps): bump drizzle 0.45 → 0.46`.

If a commit spans two scopes evenly, that's a smell — split it.

### Commit granularity — one coherent unit per commit

A phase is one or several commits. Default to **one coherent unit per commit** — a single focused change that compiles and stands on its own. The exact number of commits per phase depends on the work: a mechanical port may be one commit per phase; a feature build may be several. Optimise for "the diff tells a clear story", not for a target commit count.

Calibration:

| Granularity | Example | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| Too small | `feat: rename variable in ext-card.vue` | No — file-by-file noise |
| Right | `chore(p0): install @nuxt/eslint and base config` | Yes |
| Right | `feat(p2-db): add extension drizzle schema` | Yes |
| Right | `feat(p3-browse): ExtCard component + test` | Yes — code + its tests usually one commit |
| Right | `feat(p3-browse): wire ExtCard into home grid` | Yes |
| Right | `feat(p15-ui): port shadcn-vue primitives` covering ~28 generated wrappers (Button + Input + Label + Textarea + Checkbox + Skeleton + Dialog × 6 + Sheet × 4 + Popover × 3 + Select × 5 + Tabs × 4) | Yes — single mechanical unit |
| Too big | `feat(p3-browse): browse page` covering 8 unrelated files | No — split where the story changes |

Tests usually live with the code they cover in the same commit. The exception is adding tests to *existing* code as a coverage-improvement pass — then the test commit stands alone.

Always-split boundaries (these go in separate commits even if "small"):

- Database schema / migration vs. application code that uses it.
- Refactor vs. new feature (refactor first; feature on top).
- Generated code (migrations, lockfile updates) vs. source.
- Dependency install (`chore`) vs. first use (`feat`).

### Per-commit vs. per-PR validate

The validate suite has two gates with different strictness:

- **Each commit must compile and `bun run typecheck` must pass.** This is the per-step gate. Strict enough to keep history bisectable; loose enough that you can land a Zod validator in one commit before its consumer in the next.
- **The PR head must pass full `bun run validate`** (lint + typecheck + test + coverage thresholds). This is the per-PR gate. Enforced by the `pre-push` hook locally and by CI on the PR.
--

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p2-db): extend collections schema with slug, visibility, and description

Adds the columns user-facing collections need: a 10-char base36 shortcode
(unique, used in /collections/<shortcode> URLs), description / descriptionZh,
a private|public visibility enum, publishedAt (stamped on first
private→public transition for stable browse sort), and updatedAt.

The generated migration was hand-edited so slug is added nullable first,
backfilled via md5(id||rand()) for any pre-existing system rows, and only
then flipped to NOT NULL + UNIQUE — keeping the upgrade safe on existing
deployments. System collections (installed/saved) get shortcodes like any
other row; the UI just treats them specially.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

input.command:
git add shared/validators/collection.ts server/utils/collections/shortcode.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p9-collections): add zod validators and shortcode helper

The validators mirror shared/validators/profile.ts — `_MAX` constants so
the UI can wire inline counters, and trim+empty-string→undefined coercion
so the API doesn't need to special-case blank optional fields.

The shortcode helper draws 10 base36 chars from randomBytes with rejection
sampling to avoid modulo bias, and a small DB-checked unique wrapper that
retries up to 5 times. ~36^10 keyspace makes collisions a non-issue at any
realistic row count, but the retry keeps it bulletproof.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

git add server/repositories/collections.ts server/utils/queries/collections.ts server/utils/installs.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p9-collections): extend collections repository with user-facing CRUD

Adds the operations the new internal API needs:

- findBySlug / findById / create / update / remove / addItemBySlug /
  removeItemBySlug / membershipForExtension — all owner-scoped, with a
  typed CollectionError covering not_found / forbidden /
  system_collection_locked so the routes can map codes → HTTP statuses
  in one place.
- update stamps publishedAt on the first private→public transition and
  leaves it on flip-back so the browse sort order stays stable.
- A new view-layer module (server/utils/queries/collections.ts) carries
  the aggregated shapes the profile, browse, and detail pages need —
  itemCount via a correlated subquery, owner display name via a join.
- getOrCreateSystem now also returns slug. The new shortcode column is
  NOT NULL, so the existing recordInstall flow needs to generate one
  when it lazy-creates the user's "Installed" collection. The shape
  change ripples into installs.test.ts's mock; that's the only consumer.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

git add server/utils/collections/errors.ts server/api/internal/collections/ server/api/internal/collection-memberships.get.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p9-collections): internal API routes for collections

Adds the endpoints the UI calls:

- GET/POST /api/internal/collections
- GET /api/internal/collections/[slug]              (public→anyone,
                                                     private→owner-only;
                                                     missing rows return
                                                     404 even on direct
                                                     hits to avoid private-
                                                     vs-missing info leaks)
- POST /api/internal/collections/[slug]/update      (rename, edit copy,
                                                     visibility toggle)
- POST /api/internal/collections/[slug]/delete
- POST /api/internal/collections/[slug]/items
- POST /api/internal/collections/[slug]/items/[extensionId]/remove
- GET /api/internal/collections/public              (browse, paginated)
- GET /api/internal/collection-memberships          (powers the popover's
                                                     checkbox state)

Mutations use POST sub-paths instead of PATCH/DELETE on [slug] for two
reasons: it matches the codebase's existing convention (publish/submit.post,
profile/update.post), and Nitro's InternalApi type lookup gets confused by
.patch.ts/.delete.ts siblings, which made $fetch reject the method.

The membership lookup deliberately lives outside `collections/[slug]/` —
a sibling under it would shadow the dynamic-param route in Nitro's
generated types and force the same $fetch typing dance.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

feat(p9-collections): bookmark popover with multi-collection picker

Replaces the single-button "Save to Saved" behavior with a checkbox-list
popover anchored to the existing bookmark icon. "Saved" stays pinned at the
top so one-tap bookmarking still works; below it are the user's named
collections, plus an inline "+ New collection" creator that creates and
adds the current extension in one shot.

Toggles are optimistic — the membership set updates immediately, with a
rollback on POST failure. The bookmark icon's filled state reflects whether
the extension is in any collection.

SaveButton.vue stays as a thin wrapper around SaveToCollectionPopover so
the call sites in ExtHero etc. don't need to change.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat(collections): user-curated collections with public sharing" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Turns the existing implicit-bookmark schema (the `systemKind='saved'` collection) into a real "save and organize" surface: users create named collections (bilingual name + description), add/remove extensions, and toggle each collection between **private** and **public**. Public collections get a stable, copy-pasteable `/collections/<shortcode>` URL and appear on a `/collections` browse page.

- The bookmark button on extension pages opens a checkbox-list popover (Saved pinned at top, user collections below, inline "+ New collection") instead of a single toggle.
- Profile gains a "Collections" tab next to "Saved".
- TopBar gains a "Collections" nav entry.
- Web scope only — `/api/v1` is **untouched** so the CLI keeps working unchanged. CLI install-collection is deferred per the plan.

## Schema

`shared/db/schema/collection.ts` gains `slug` (10-char base36, unique), `description`/`descriptionZh`, `visibility` enum (`private` | `public`), `publishedAt` (stamped on first private→public flip), `updatedAt`, plus indexes on `ownerUserId` and `(visibility, publishedAt DESC)`.

Migration `drizzle/0007_wakeful_cloak.sql` was hand-edited to add `slug` nullable → backfill via `md5(id||rand())` → flip to NOT NULL + UNIQUE so the upgrade is safe on existing rows.

## API surface (internal)

| Route | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `GET /api/internal/collections` | Owner list; lazy-seeds Saved |
| `POST /api/internal/collections` | Create |
| `GET /api/internal/collections/[slug]` | Public to anyone, private to owner only |
| `POST /api/internal/collections/[slug]/update` | Rename / edit copy / visibility toggle |
| `POST /api/internal/collections/[slug]/delete` | Owner-only; system collections return 422 |
| `POST /api/internal/collections/[slug]/items` | Add extension (idempotent) |
| `POST /api/internal/collections/[slug]/items/[extensionId]/remove` | Remove |
| `GET /api/internal/collections/public` | Paginated browse |
| `GET /api/internal/collection-memberships?extensionId=…` | Powers the popover checkbox state |

`/api/v1` is **unchanged**.

## Pivotal decisions

1. **Two-state visibility** (`private` | `public`). Unlisted deferred — the enum can grow without a migration if needed.
2. **`/collections/<shortcode>`** URLs, same path for public + private; visitors hitting a private one get a 404 (no info leak vs. truly missing).
3. **POST-based mutations** instead of PATCH/DELETE on `[slug]` — matches the codebase's existing `publish/submit.post` convention and sidesteps a Nitro InternalApi type collision that broke `$fetch` method typing.
4. **System "Saved" stays.** The picker pins it at the top; the existing recordInstall flow still writes to it via the shared `getOrCreateSystem` helper (now also returning the slug it generated).

## Test plan

End-to-end was already verified against a running dev server:

- [x] `bun run db:migrate` against the existing local DB — clean, journal updated.
- [x] Sign-up → list collections (lazy-seeded Saved row appears) → create a public collection → add `ext-1` → owner GET shows `itemCount:1`.
- [x] Anonymous GET on the public collection returns 200; after `update {visibility:"private"}` returns 404.
- [x] `publishedAt` stays populated across visibility toggles (stable browse sort).
- [x] `POST .../[saved-slug]/delete` returns 422 with `system_collection_locked`.
- [x] `/en/collections` and `/en/collections/[slug]` SSR-render correctly.
- [x] `/zh/collections/[slug]` shows bilingual `nameZh` + Chinese visibility chip.
- [x] `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + 242/242 tests) green.

Still to do post-merge:
- [ ] Browser-level smoke test (popover click, "+ New collection", visibility toggle, copy-link affordance).
- [ ] Add `server/utils/queries/collections.test.ts` and `shared/validators/collection.test.ts` integration coverage.
- [ ] Decide whether to fold the existing "Saved" tab into the new "Collections" tab in a follow-up.

## Out of scope (deferred)

- No `/api/v1/collections/*` public registry endpoints. CLI cannot install-by-collection yet.
- No org/department-scoped collections (only user-owned). `ownerOrgId` can be added later.
- No featured/curated collections on the home page.
- No fork, rating, or comments on collections.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
